.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:7012:### Finding 2: The “4-layer stack” is mostly an operating scaffold, not a novel retrieval engine
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:7020:### Finding 3: “Store everything” is real, but ingest is still routed, chunked, and sometimes extracted
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:7028:### Finding 4: The diary pattern is useful, but its current storage plane is architecturally messy

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:1:### Finding 4: Engram treats user prompts as a first-class retrieval lane, not just raw context input
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:8:### Finding 5: Engram’s thin runtime adapters do real safety work: sub-agent suppression, passive capture, and compaction instructions
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:15:### Finding 6: Topic keys are surfaced as an agent workflow, not just a storage field
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:238:# Iteration 004: SESSION LIFECYCLE
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:243:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:245:### Finding N: [Title]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7051:# Iteration 004: SESSION LIFECYCLE
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7056:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7057:### Finding 1: Engram makes session creation opportunistic at every durable ingress, not just at explicit start
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7064:### Finding 2: Engram splits session closure into two persistence lanes: lightweight session metadata and searchable narrative memory
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7071:### Finding 3: Engram’s recovery path is simple and deterministic because `FormatContext()` assembles a bounded session package
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7078:### Finding 4: Engram treats session rows, prompts, and summaries as sync/export entities, so cross-session continuity is portable rather than purely local
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7085:### Finding 5: Public is stronger on trustworthy recovery, while Engram is stronger on explicit, agent-agnostic session lifecycle APIs
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7126:# Iteration 004: SESSION LIFECYCLE

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7139:### Finding 2: Engram splits session closure into two persistence lanes: lightweight session metadata and searchable narrative memory
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7146:### Finding 3: Engram’s recovery path is simple and deterministic because `FormatContext()` assembles a bounded session package
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7153:### Finding 4: Engram treats session rows, prompts, and summaries as sync/export entities, so cross-session continuity is portable rather than purely local
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:7160:### Finding 5: Public is stronger on trustworthy recovery, while Engram is stronger on explicit, agent-agnostic session lifecycle APIs
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:182:# Iteration 001: ARCHITECTURE OVERVIEW

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5911:### Finding 1: Mex uses a router-first markdown scaffold as its control plane
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5918:### Finding 2: The CLI is a thin orchestrator over distinct setup, drift, scan, sync, pattern, and hook subsystems
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5925:### Finding 3: Mex front-loads static scanning so AI writes from a structured brief instead of raw repo exploration
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5932:### Finding 4: The drift engine is a clean static-analysis pipeline, not an AI validator
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5939:### Finding 5: Mex’s repair layer is issue-scoped and surgical, with filesystem and git evidence bundled into the prompt
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5975:# Iteration 001: ARCHITECTURE OVERVIEW

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5988:### Finding 2: The CLI is a thin orchestrator over distinct setup, drift, scan, sync, pattern, and hook subsystems
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5995:### Finding 3: Mex front-loads static scanning so AI writes from a structured brief instead of raw repo exploration
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:6002:### Finding 4: The drift engine is a clean static-analysis pipeline, not an AI validator
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:6009:### Finding 5: Mex’s repair layer is issue-scoped and surgical, with filesystem and git evidence bundled into the prompt
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:213:# Iteration 001: ARCHITECTURE OVERVIEW

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10442:### Finding 1: MemPalace is really four subsystems around one shared local store boundary
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10449:### Finding 2: The raw-verbatim posture is source-confirmed, but “closets” are mostly product language in this version
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10456:### Finding 3: Agent discipline is embedded into the MCP surface, not left to prompts alone
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10463:### Finding 4: The wake-up stack is real, but the implementation is simpler than the README framing
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10470:### Finding 5: Palace graph traversal and temporal KG are separate systems with different truth models
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10477:### Finding 6: Ingestion is not “store everything untouched”; it is chunk-and-route with heuristic restructuring on some paths
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10484:### Finding 7: Hook-driven preservation is a timing architecture, not a storage architecture
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10491:### Finding 8: Public already beats MemPalace on session bootstrap sophistication, but MemPalace beats Public on memory protocol ergonomics
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10538:# Iteration 001: ARCHITECTURE OVERVIEW

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10551:### Finding 2: The raw-verbatim posture is source-confirmed, but “closets” are mostly product language in this version
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10558:### Finding 3: Agent discipline is embedded into the MCP surface, not left to prompts alone
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10565:### Finding 4: The wake-up stack is real, but the implementation is simpler than the README framing
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10572:### Finding 5: Palace graph traversal and temporal KG are separate systems with different truth models
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10579:### Finding 6: Ingestion is not “store everything untouched”; it is chunk-and-route with heuristic restructuring on some paths
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10586:### Finding 7: Hook-driven preservation is a timing architecture, not a storage architecture
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10593:### Finding 8: Public already beats MemPalace on session bootstrap sophistication, but MemPalace beats Public on memory protocol ergonomics
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:195:# Iteration 013: GAP ANALYSIS - PARADIGM SHIFTS

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:8499:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:8500:### Finding 1: Split “memory health” into storage integrity vs knowledge-truth drift
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:8507:### Finding 2: Freshness should include repository-activity drift, not just session and graph readiness
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:8514:### Finding 3: Repair should be turned into bounded, file-scoped remediation packets
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:8521:### Finding 4: Durable project knowledge wants a doc-native routing scaffold beside indexed memory
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:8528:### Finding 5: Pattern growth is worth keeping, but only with a higher authority bar than Mex uses
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:8561:# Iteration 013: GAP ANALYSIS - PARADIGM SHIFTS
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:8566:## Findings

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:8574:### Finding 2: Freshness should include repository-activity drift, not just session and graph readiness
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:8581:### Finding 3: Repair should be turned into bounded, file-scoped remediation packets
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:8588:### Finding 4: Durable project knowledge wants a doc-native routing scaffold beside indexed memory
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:8595:### Finding 5: Pattern growth is worth keeping, but only with a higher authority bar than Mex uses
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:1:### Finding 4: The diary pattern is useful, but its current storage plane is architecturally messy

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:9804:# Iteration 015: DEEP DIVE - SECOND PATTERN

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:9818:### Finding 2: The KG’s core mechanic is append-plus-invalidate temporal truth, not automatic contradiction resolution
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:9826:### Finding 3: MemPalace turns the fact plane into agent behavior by embedding KG discipline in the status bootstrap
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:9834:### Finding 4: Public already has a partial analogue, but it stops at memory lineage rather than first-class facts
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:9842:### Finding 5: MemPalace’s fact plane is useful, but its provenance and hygiene are too loose for direct adoption
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:9850:### Finding 6: Palace graph and temporal KG are intentionally different systems, and Public should keep that separation too
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:9898:# Iteration 015: DEEP DIVE - SECOND PATTERN

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:9912:### Finding 2: The KG’s core mechanic is append-plus-invalidate temporal truth, not automatic contradiction resolution
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:9920:### Finding 3: MemPalace turns the fact plane into agent behavior by embedding KG discipline in the status bootstrap

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:11998:### Finding 5: The diary is MemPalace’s only explicit across-session summary lane, and it is agent-scoped rather than session-scoped

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9250:### Finding 1: Mex does not implement live ranked retrieval; it implements deterministic scaffold navigation
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9257:### Finding 2: Mex’s strongest bootstrap retrieval idea is the bounded scanner brief, but it is shallow and heuristic

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9271:### Finding 4: Mex’s `triggers` are scaffold metadata, not an executable recall mechanism

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9324:# Iteration 008: COMPARISON - RETRIEVAL

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9337:### Finding 2: Mex’s strongest bootstrap retrieval idea is the bounded scanner brief, but it is shallow and heuristic
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9344:### Finding 3: Mex’s repair-time retrieval is issue-seeded, file-scoped, and more surgical than its general navigation model
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9351:### Finding 4: Mex’s `triggers` are scaffold metadata, not an executable recall mechanism
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9358:### Finding 5: Spec Kit Memory is a real hybrid retrieval engine; Mex is not in the same class
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9365:### Finding 6: Our semantic-to-structural bridge is the sharpest retrieval advantage over Mex
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:1:### Finding 4: Command And Dependency Parity Checks Need A Broader Inventory Model Than Mex Has
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:8:### Finding 5: Drift-To-Repair Briefing Is Promising, But It Should Become A Spec Kit Repair Surface, Not A Mex-Style CLI Loop
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:234:# Iteration 018: RISK ASSESSMENT

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7215:### Finding 1: Static Reference Integrity Is Worth Adopting, But Mex’s Path Semantics Are Too Shallow To Port Directly
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7222:### Finding 2: Git-Based Freshness Helps, But Silent Nulls And Dual Thresholds Could Distort The Signal
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7229:### Finding 3: Command, Dependency, And Script Parity Would Break Down Quickly In A Multi-Surface Repo
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7236:### Finding 4: Drift-To-Repair Briefing Is Reusable, But Mex’s Sync Loop Is Too Optimistic For Spec Kit
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7243:### Finding 5: Auto-Growth Is Powerful, But In Spec Kit It Could Turn Partial Knowledge Into Durable Policy
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7250:### Finding 6: Mex’s Single Drift Score Would Be Misleading If We Treated It As A Primary Health Signal

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:8668:# Iteration 007: AGENT INTEGRATION
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:8673:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:8674:### Finding 1: Mex standardizes agent onboarding around one shared scaffold anchor
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:8681:### Finding 2: Mex narrows agent scope during setup by forcing a synthesized brief before broad exploration
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:8688:### Finding 3: Mex’s sync flow is a surgical repair generator, not a generic “rewrite docs” loop
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:8695:### Finding 4: Mex has almost no real multi-agent safety beyond prompts telling one agent to behave
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:8702:### Finding 5: Mex’s “passive capture” is really passive drift nudging, not passive knowledge capture
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:8709:### Finding 6: Mex enforces repo-root and scaffold-root boundaries, but only at the project level
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:8743:# Iteration 007: AGENT INTEGRATION

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:8756:### Finding 2: Mex narrows agent scope during setup by forcing a synthesized brief before broad exploration
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:8763:### Finding 3: Mex’s sync flow is a surgical repair generator, not a generic “rewrite docs” loop
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:8770:### Finding 4: Mex has almost no real multi-agent safety beyond prompts telling one agent to behave
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:8777:### Finding 5: Mex’s “passive capture” is really passive drift nudging, not passive knowledge capture

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:271:# Iteration 006: MEMORY HYGIENE

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:11849:# Iteration 006: MEMORY HYGIENE
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:11854:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:11855:### Finding 1: MemPalace hygiene is mostly bolt-on cleanup, not an integrated write model
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:11862:### Finding 2: Project re-mines are delete-and-reinsert, convo re-mines are deterministic upserts, but neither path preserves drawer lineage
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:11869:### Finding 3: Real fact revision tracking exists only in the SQLite knowledge graph, not in the drawer store
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:11876:### Finding 4: Topic-key stability is weaker than the README suggests and can fragment the palace over time
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:11883:### Finding 5: Deletion and garbage collection are hard-delete maintenance paths with audit hints, not soft-delete lifecycle controls
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:11890:### Finding 6: Public already has the stronger hygiene architecture: append-only lineage, archival supersession, immutable audit, and logical-key repair
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:11942:# Iteration 006: MEMORY HYGIENE

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:11955:### Finding 2: Project re-mines are delete-and-reinsert, convo re-mines are deterministic upserts, but neither path preserves drawer lineage
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:11962:### Finding 3: Real fact revision tracking exists only in the SQLite knowledge graph, not in the drawer store
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:11969:### Finding 4: Topic-key stability is weaker than the README suggests and can fragment the palace over time

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:1:### Finding 3: Mex’s repair-time retrieval is issue-seeded, file-scoped, and more surgical than its general navigation model

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:22:### Finding 6: Our semantic-to-structural bridge is the sharpest retrieval advantage over Mex
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:238:# Iteration 009: COMPARISON - LIFECYCLE

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9292:### Finding 3: Mex has a strong repair lifecycle loop even though it lacks a true session subsystem
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9299:### Finding 4: Spec Kit Memory has a trusted, scoped session model; Mex effectively has none

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9354:# Iteration 009: COMPARISON - LIFECYCLE

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9368:### Finding 2: Mex’s `init`/`setup` flow creates a cold-start brief, but it is not continuity state
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9375:### Finding 3: Mex has a strong repair lifecycle loop even though it lacks a true session subsystem
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:9382:### Finding 4: Spec Kit Memory has a trusted, scoped session model; Mex effectively has none

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:8:### Finding 6: Public already has the stronger lifecycle architecture; MemPalace mainly contributes selective ideas, not a better overall model
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:265:# Iteration 005: SEARCH MECHANISMS

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:16292:### Finding 5: Public already has the stronger live retrieval stack: multi-channel search, lexical fallback, and richer score fusion

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:16345:### Finding 2: MemPalace’s main “shortcuts” are taxonomy narrowing and ingest-time room assignment, not query-time hybrid search
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:16352:### Finding 3: Wake-up relevance is static importance sorting, and L2 recall is raw filtered fetch, not scored retrieval
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:16359:### Finding 4: The interesting hybrid and rerank logic exists in benchmark code, not in the shipped product surface
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:16366:### Finding 5: Public already has the stronger live retrieval stack: multi-channel search, lexical fallback, and richer score fusion
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:1:### Finding 6: Spec Kit Memory is structurally in a different class, so Mex should complement it rather than replace it
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:208:# Iteration 003: TOOL/API SURFACE
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:213:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:215:### Finding N: [Title]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:6494:# Iteration 003: TOOL/API SURFACE
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:6499:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:6501:### Finding 1: Mex exposes a CLI contract, not a tool protocol
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:6508:### Finding 2: Mex’s “profile separation” is instruction-file distribution, not capability separation
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:6515:### Finding 3: Mex’s repair loop is single-provider and interactive by construction
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:6522:### Finding 4: Mex’s own command surface already drifts in code
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:6529:### Finding 5: Mex’s registration model is small, explicit, and worth borrowing for a docs-drift subsystem
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:6536:### Finding 6: Public already has the large, layered surface that most needs Mex-style static drift checks
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:6585:# Iteration 003: TOOL/API SURFACE
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:6590:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:6592:### Finding 1: Mex exposes a CLI contract, not a tool protocol
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:6599:### Finding 2: Mex’s “profile separation” is instruction-file distribution, not capability separation
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:6606:### Finding 3: Mex’s repair loop is single-provider and interactive by construction
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:6613:### Finding 4: Mex’s own command surface already drifts in code
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:6620:### Finding 5: Mex’s registration model is small, explicit, and worth borrowing for a docs-drift subsystem
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:6627:### Finding 6: Public already has the large, layered surface that most needs Mex-style static drift checks
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:1:### Finding 5: We should add reverse coverage audits that detect live repo knowledge missing from memory/spec surfaces
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:238:# Iteration 012: GAP ANALYSIS - REFACTORS
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:243:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:245:### Finding N: [Title]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:9405:# Iteration 012: GAP ANALYSIS - REFACTORS
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:9410:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:9411:### Finding 1: We should split our current “health” surface into storage health and knowledge-drift health
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:9418:### Finding 2: We should add a separate repo-activity freshness signal instead of overloading session/code-graph freshness
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:9425:### Finding 3: Our repair architecture should emit structured issue bundles, not just hints plus internal auto-repair actions
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:9432:### Finding 4: We should reject Mex’s heuristic markdown-claim extraction as a primary authority model
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:9439:### Finding 5: If we add a pattern layer, it must be provenance-backed and quality-gated from creation time
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:9446:### Finding 6: A single numeric “knowledge health score” could help operators, but only as a secondary surface
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:9505:# Iteration 012: GAP ANALYSIS - REFACTORS
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:9510:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:9511:### Finding 1: We should split our current “health” surface into storage health and knowledge-drift health
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:9518:### Finding 2: We should add a separate repo-activity freshness signal instead of overloading session/code-graph freshness
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:9525:### Finding 3: Our repair architecture should emit structured issue bundles, not just hints plus internal auto-repair actions
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:9532:### Finding 4: We should reject Mex’s heuristic markdown-claim extraction as a primary authority model
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:9539:### Finding 5: If we add a pattern layer, it must be provenance-backed and quality-gated from creation time
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:9546:### Finding 6: A single numeric “knowledge health score” could help operators, but only as a secondary surface
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:1:### Finding 4: MemPalace session state is intentionally light; Public already has the stronger crash-recovery and trusted-session model
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:8:### Finding 5: MemPalace leaves save-time organization to the agent or heuristics; Public’s structured save contract is more auditable and safer
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:254:# Iteration 010: COMPARISON - HYGIENE
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:259:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:261:### Finding N: [Title]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10781:# Iteration 010: COMPARISON - HYGIENE
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10786:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10787:### Finding 1: MemPalace duplicate hygiene is a thin similarity check; Public treats duplicates as a scoped save-integrity problem
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10794:### Finding 2: MemPalace retrieval hygiene relies on agent obedience; Public encodes more hygiene directly into trigger recall
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10801:### Finding 3: MemPalace health tooling is mostly “count and rebuild”; Public has bounded, confirmation-gated repair hygiene
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10808:### Finding 4: MemPalace’s temporal KG is more expressive than our causal links, but its hygiene is manual and not contradiction-safe yet
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10815:### Finding 5: MemPalace’s “store everything” story is selectively lossy in the ingest pipeline, which weakens its hygiene claims
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10822:### Finding 6: MemPalace diary storage knowingly trades recall hygiene for compression convenience
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10857:# Iteration 010: COMPARISON - HYGIENE
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10862:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10863:### Finding 1: MemPalace duplicate hygiene is a thin similarity check; Public treats duplicates as a scoped save-integrity problem
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10870:### Finding 2: MemPalace retrieval hygiene relies on agent obedience; Public encodes more hygiene directly into trigger recall
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10877:### Finding 3: MemPalace health tooling is mostly “count and rebuild”; Public has bounded, confirmation-gated repair hygiene
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10884:### Finding 4: MemPalace’s temporal KG is more expressive than our causal links, but its hygiene is manual and not contradiction-safe yet
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10891:### Finding 5: MemPalace’s “store everything” story is selectively lossy in the ingest pipeline, which weakens its hygiene claims
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10898:### Finding 6: MemPalace diary storage knowingly trades recall hygiene for compression convenience
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:239:# Iteration 017: INTEGRATION FEASIBILITY
