.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:1:### Finding 5: Public already has the stronger safety architecture for session continuity, scoped retrieval, and compaction transport
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:258:# Iteration 008: COMPARISON - RETRIEVAL
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:263:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:265:### Finding N: [Title]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:5273:# Iteration 008: COMPARISON - RETRIEVAL
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:5278:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:5279:### Finding 1: Shipped MemPalace retrieval is still a flat single-collection Chroma query; the stronger hybrid/rerank story mostly lives in benchmark paths
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:5286:### Finding 2: Wing and room retrieval are useful browse handles, but L2 is metadata filtering, not a competing retrieval engine
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:5293:### Finding 3: The L0/L1/L2/L3 “wake-up stack” is mostly presentation policy over one store, not four distinct retrieval substrates
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:5300:### Finding 4: Palace graph traversal is topic-neighborhood navigation from metadata, not a substitute for code-graph or causal retrieval
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:5307:### Finding 5: Public’s retrieval stack is already better factored and more inspectable than MemPalace’s single-surface search
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:5349:# Iteration 008: COMPARISON - RETRIEVAL
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:5354:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:5355:### Finding 1: Shipped MemPalace retrieval is still a flat single-collection Chroma query; the stronger hybrid/rerank story mostly lives in benchmark paths
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:5362:### Finding 2: Wing and room retrieval are useful browse handles, but L2 is metadata filtering, not a competing retrieval engine
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:5369:### Finding 3: The L0/L1/L2/L3 “wake-up stack” is mostly presentation policy over one store, not four distinct retrieval substrates
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:5376:### Finding 4: Palace graph traversal is topic-neighborhood navigation from metadata, not a substitute for code-graph or causal retrieval
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:5383:### Finding 5: Public’s retrieval stack is already better factored and more inspectable than MemPalace’s single-surface search
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:1:### Finding 4: Public already has a partial analogue, but it stops at memory lineage rather than first-class facts
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:9:### Finding 5: MemPalace’s fact plane is useful, but its provenance and hygiene are too loose for direct adoption
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:17:### Finding 6: Palace graph and temporal KG are intentionally different systems, and Public should keep that separation too
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:273:# Iteration 016: EDGE CASES & FAILURE MODES
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:278:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:280:### Finding N: [Title]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11159:# Iteration 016: EDGE CASES & FAILURE MODES
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11164:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11165:### Finding 1: `mempalace_add_drawer` can silently alias distinct memories instead of truly deduplicating them
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11172:### Finding 2: migration and repair are first-class concepts, but the live recovery path is still heavy and partially fragmented
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11179:### Finding 3: health and taxonomy surfaces prefer “return something” over “return the whole truth”
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11186:### Finding 4: hook-driven preservation is useful, but its timeout and parsing envelope is brittle
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11193:### Finding 5: the temporal KG is operational, but truth maintenance under concurrency is weak
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11200:### Finding 6: “store everything” is still bounded by silent skips, lossy reads, and heuristic side paths
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11257:# Iteration 016: EDGE CASES & FAILURE MODES
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11262:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11263:### Finding 1: `mempalace_add_drawer` can silently alias distinct memories instead of truly deduplicating them
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11270:### Finding 2: migration and repair are first-class concepts, but the live recovery path is still heavy and partially fragmented
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11277:### Finding 3: health and taxonomy surfaces prefer “return something” over “return the whole truth”
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11284:### Finding 4: hook-driven preservation is useful, but its timeout and parsing envelope is brittle
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11291:### Finding 5: the temporal KG is operational, but truth maintenance under concurrency is weak
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11298:### Finding 6: “store everything” is still bounded by silent skips, lossy reads, and heuristic side paths
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:1:### Finding 3: Engram’s lexical layer is a minimal trigger-synced FTS shadow; Public already evolved that primitive far beyond raw `fts.rank`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8:### Finding 4: Engram’s row-overwrite revision model and Public’s append-only lineage solve different problems
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:15:### Finding 5: Engram bakes replication backlog into the core store; for Public that is packaging, not a foundational memory primitive
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:22:### Finding 6: Public has already added compaction-survival tables that Engram does not have, especially summaries and active projection
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:241:# Iteration 003: TOOL/API SURFACE
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:246:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:248:### Finding N: [Title]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:9774:# Iteration 003: TOOL/API SURFACE
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:9779:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:9780:### Finding 1: Engram’s profile split is a real registration boundary, and setup always installs the agent-safe slice
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:9787:### Finding 2: Engram has a second layer of surface control: “core” eager tools and deferred drill-in tools
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:9794:### Finding 3: Engram exposes explicit session lifecycle APIs end to end; Public mostly keeps lifecycle implicit behind trusted session IDs
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:9801:### Finding 4: Engram treats user prompts as a first-class retrieval lane, not just raw context input
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:9808:### Finding 5: Engram’s thin runtime adapters do real safety work: sub-agent suppression, passive capture, and compaction instructions
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:9815:### Finding 6: Topic keys are surfaced as an agent workflow, not just a storage field
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:9860:# Iteration 003: TOOL/API SURFACE
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:9865:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:9866:### Finding 1: Engram’s profile split is a real registration boundary, and setup always installs the agent-safe slice
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:9873:### Finding 2: Engram has a second layer of surface control: “core” eager tools and deferred drill-in tools
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:9880:### Finding 3: Engram exposes explicit session lifecycle APIs end to end; Public mostly keeps lifecycle implicit behind trusted session IDs
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:9887:### Finding 4: Engram treats user prompts as a first-class retrieval lane, not just raw context input
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:9894:### Finding 5: Engram’s thin runtime adapters do real safety work: sub-agent suppression, passive capture, and compaction instructions
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:9901:### Finding 6: Topic keys are surfaced as an agent workflow, not just a storage field
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:1:### Finding 6: Recovery design treats `chroma.sqlite3` as the durable source-of-truth and HNSW as disposable
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:9:### Finding 7: Public already has the stronger normalized storage model; MemPalace’s reusable value is a raw-verbatim sidecar, not a backend replacement
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:253:# Iteration 003: TOOL/API SURFACE
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:258:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:260:### Finding N: [Title]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8403:# Iteration 003: TOOL/API SURFACE
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8408:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8409:### Finding 1: The real CLI surface is broader than the README, and `init` does not execute the guided onboarding path it advertises
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8416:### Finding 2: MemPalace’s MCP server is a thin stdin/stdout JSON-RPC transport with a single flat tool registry
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8423:### Finding 3: `mempalace_status` is not just health output; it is an embedded operating protocol for the agent
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8430:### Finding 4: Host/profile separation is shallow in source; multi-client support is partly documentation, not runtime implementation
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8437:### Finding 5: The duplicate-check API is advisory, while write correctness is left to caller discipline
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8444:### Finding 6: MemPalace’s wake-up/search layers are real, but they are API-adjacent utilities rather than first-class MCP profiles
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8477:# Iteration 003: TOOL/API SURFACE
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8482:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8483:### Finding 1: The real CLI surface is broader than the README, and `init` does not execute the guided onboarding path it advertises
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8490:### Finding 2: MemPalace’s MCP server is a thin stdin/stdout JSON-RPC transport with a single flat tool registry
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8497:### Finding 3: `mempalace_status` is not just health output; it is an embedded operating protocol for the agent
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8504:### Finding 4: Host/profile separation is shallow in source; multi-client support is partly documentation, not runtime implementation
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8511:### Finding 5: The duplicate-check API is advisory, while write correctness is left to caller discipline
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8518:### Finding 6: MemPalace’s wake-up/search layers are real, but they are API-adjacent utilities rather than first-class MCP profiles
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:1:### Finding 5: Deletion and garbage collection are hard-delete maintenance paths with audit hints, not soft-delete lifecycle controls
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:8:### Finding 6: Public already has the stronger hygiene architecture: append-only lineage, archival supersession, immutable audit, and logical-key repair
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:268:# Iteration 007: AGENT INTEGRATION
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:273:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:275:### Finding N: [Title]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10555:# Iteration 007: AGENT INTEGRATION
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10560:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10561:### Finding 1: `mempalace_status` is a behavior bootstrap, not just a health check
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10568:### Finding 2: Passive capture is genuinely agent-agnostic in concept, but classification safety is delegated back to the agent
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10575:### Finding 3: “Specialist agents” are implemented as separate diary wings, not as a real multi-agent safety model
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10582:### Finding 4: Project scoping is ergonomic metadata routing, not governed scope control
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10589:### Finding 5: Public already has the stronger safety architecture for session continuity, scoped retrieval, and compaction transport
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10638:# Iteration 007: AGENT INTEGRATION
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10643:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10644:### Finding 1: `mempalace_status` is a behavior bootstrap, not just a health check
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10651:### Finding 2: Passive capture is genuinely agent-agnostic in concept, but classification safety is delegated back to the agent
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10658:### Finding 3: “Specialist agents” are implemented as separate diary wings, not as a real multi-agent safety model
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10665:### Finding 4: Project scoping is ergonomic metadata routing, not governed scope control
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10672:### Finding 5: Public already has the stronger safety architecture for session continuity, scoped retrieval, and compaction transport
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:1:### Finding 4: Public should reject a separate L0/L1/L2/L3 engine and express wake-up behavior as profiles on the existing retrieval stack
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:8:### Finding 5: Public should preserve its modular handler architecture and not absorb new memory features into a single giant server file
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:266:# Iteration 013: GAP ANALYSIS - PARADIGM SHIFTS
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:271:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:273:### Finding N: [Title]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9729:# Iteration 013: GAP ANALYSIS - PARADIGM SHIFTS
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9734:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9735:### Finding 1: Memory becomes a lifecycle policy, not just a retrieval API
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9742:### Finding 2: Context delivery should be a response compiler, not a separate wake-up stack
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9749:### Finding 3: A strong memory system can have multiple planes of truth without forcing them into one index
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9756:### Finding 4: Authority surfaces matter more than feature count
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9763:### Finding 5: Identity should be governed internally and human-friendly externally
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9770:### Finding 6: Truthfulness boundaries are part of the architecture
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9806:# Iteration 013: GAP ANALYSIS - PARADIGM SHIFTS
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9811:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9812:### Finding 1: Memory becomes a lifecycle policy, not just a retrieval API
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9819:### Finding 2: Context delivery should be a response compiler, not a separate wake-up stack
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9826:### Finding 3: A strong memory system can have multiple planes of truth without forcing them into one index
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9833:### Finding 4: Authority surfaces matter more than feature count
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9840:### Finding 5: Identity should be governed internally and human-friendly externally
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9847:### Finding 6: Truthfulness boundaries are part of the architecture
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:1:### Finding 4: MemPalace ships a browse-first taxonomy surface; Public is still mostly query-first
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:8:### Finding 5: Public is missing topic-bridge navigation across memory domains
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:271:# Iteration 012: GAP ANALYSIS - REFACTORS
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:276:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:278:### Finding N: [Title]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:1016:### Finding 4: MemPalace’s temporal KG is more expressive than our causal links, but its hygiene is manual and not contradiction-safe yet
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:1023:### Finding 5: MemPalace’s “store everything” story is selectively lossy in the ingest pipeline, which weakens its hygiene claims
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:1030:### Finding 6: MemPalace diary storage knowingly trades recall hygiene for compression convenience
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:7591:### Finding 4: MemPalace’s temporal KG is more expressive than our causal links, but its hygiene is manual and not contradiction-safe yet
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:7598:### Finding 5: MemPalace’s “store everything” story is selectively lossy in the ingest pipeline, which weakens its hygiene claims
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:7605:### Finding 6: MemPalace diary storage knowingly trades recall hygiene for compression convenience
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:7896:# Iteration 012: GAP ANALYSIS - REFACTORS
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:7901:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:7903:### Finding 1: Public should consolidate bootstrap authority instead of adding a parallel wake-up subsystem
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:7910:### Finding 2: Public should shift compaction resilience from transport enrichment toward enforced save checkpoints
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:7917:### Finding 3: Taxonomy should stay a browse-and-narrow aid, not become the core retrieval router
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:7924:### Finding 4: Public should reject a separate L0/L1/L2/L3 engine and express wake-up behavior as profiles on the existing retrieval stack
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:7931:### Finding 5: Public should preserve its modular handler architecture and not absorb new memory features into a single giant server file
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:7981:# Iteration 012: GAP ANALYSIS - REFACTORS
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:7986:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:7988:### Finding 1: Public should consolidate bootstrap authority instead of adding a parallel wake-up subsystem
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:7995:### Finding 2: Public should shift compaction resilience from transport enrichment toward enforced save checkpoints
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:8002:### Finding 3: Taxonomy should stay a browse-and-narrow aid, not become the core retrieval router
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:8009:### Finding 4: Public should reject a separate L0/L1/L2/L3 engine and express wake-up behavior as profiles on the existing retrieval stack
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:8016:### Finding 5: Public should preserve its modular handler architecture and not absorb new memory features into a single giant server file
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:1:### Finding 3: The L0/L1/L2/L3 “wake-up stack” is mostly presentation policy over one store, not four distinct retrieval substrates
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8:### Finding 4: Palace graph traversal is topic-neighborhood navigation from metadata, not a substitute for code-graph or causal retrieval
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:15:### Finding 5: Public’s retrieval stack is already better factored and more inspectable than MemPalace’s single-surface search
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:265:# Iteration 009: COMPARISON - LIFECYCLE
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:270:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:272:### Finding N: [Title]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8025:# Iteration 009: COMPARISON - LIFECYCLE
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8030:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8031:### Finding 1: MemPalace uses `status` as an agent-behavior bootstrap, while Public spreads the same job across richer recovery surfaces
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8038:### Finding 2: MemPalace’s wake-up stack is mostly prompt packaging; Public’s resume path is materially richer state management
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8045:### Finding 3: MemPalace’s strongest lifecycle contribution is forced save timing before loss, not better saved-state structure
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8052:### Finding 4: MemPalace session state is intentionally light; Public already has the stronger crash-recovery and trusted-session model
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8059:### Finding 5: MemPalace leaves save-time organization to the agent or heuristics; Public’s structured save contract is more auditable and safer
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8097:# Iteration 009: COMPARISON - LIFECYCLE
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8102:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8103:### Finding 1: MemPalace uses `status` as an agent-behavior bootstrap, while Public spreads the same job across richer recovery surfaces
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8110:### Finding 2: MemPalace’s wake-up stack is mostly prompt packaging; Public’s resume path is materially richer state management
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8117:### Finding 3: MemPalace’s strongest lifecycle contribution is forced save timing before loss, not better saved-state structure
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8124:### Finding 4: MemPalace session state is intentionally light; Public already has the stronger crash-recovery and trusted-session model
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8131:### Finding 5: MemPalace leaves save-time organization to the agent or heuristics; Public’s structured save contract is more auditable and safer
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:1:### Finding 6: “store everything” is still bounded by silent skips, lossy reads, and heuristic side paths
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:266:# Iteration 017: INTEGRATION FEASIBILITY
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:271:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:273:### Finding N: [Title]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5320:# Iteration 017: INTEGRATION FEASIBILITY
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5325:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5327:### Finding 1: Hook-enforced pre-compaction preservation is the strongest transferable pattern
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5334:### Finding 2: Status-as-protocol bootstrap is the easiest thing to port safely
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5341:### Finding 3: The L0/L1/L2/L3 stack is reusable only as a thin digest, not as a full model
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5348:### Finding 4: Full palace taxonomy is expensive to integrate and not differentiated enough
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5355:### Finding 5: A separate temporal fact graph is valuable, but only behind a stricter source contract than MemPalace uses
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5404:# Iteration 017: INTEGRATION FEASIBILITY
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5409:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5411:### Finding 1: Hook-enforced pre-compaction preservation is the strongest transferable pattern
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5418:### Finding 2: Status-as-protocol bootstrap is the easiest thing to port safely
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5425:### Finding 3: The L0/L1/L2/L3 stack is reusable only as a thin digest, not as a full model
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5432:### Finding 4: Full palace taxonomy is expensive to integrate and not differentiated enough
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5439:### Finding 5: A separate temporal fact graph is valuable, but only behind a stricter source contract than MemPalace uses

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15592:### Finding 1: Engram is built as a store-centered hub with thin delivery surfaces
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15599:### Finding 2: Engram explicitly separates agent-safe MCP tools from admin/curation tools
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15606:### Finding 3: Engram treats sessions, prompts, and summaries as first-class persisted objects
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15613:### Finding 4: Engram’s memory hygiene revolves around stable topic threads plus duplicate suppression
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15620:### Finding 5: Engram’s search is intentionally simple: direct topic-key shortcut first, sanitized FTS5 second

 succeeded in 0ms:
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:7:### Finding 1: Modus’s MCP health surface is thinner than advertised, and thinner than Public’s actual hygiene control plane
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:14:### Finding 2: Modus’s `doctor` has one reusable hygiene idea Public does not fully cover yet: content-level contradiction and duplicate-fact linting
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:21:### Finding 3: Modus cross-references are associative adjacency maps, not a causal-memory hygiene system
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:28:### Finding 4: Modus has no trigger-recall plane; its query hygiene stops at query expansion
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:35:### Finding 5: Modus’s markdown pipeline is intentionally permissive, but that permissiveness weakens authoritative memory hygiene
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:8:### Finding 1: Modus has no first-class session object; a “session” is just process startup plus a disk-backed vault snapshot
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:15:### Finding 2: Live-session maintenance is snapshot-based, so Modus does not really “learn during the current session” without restart
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:22:### Finding 3: The only session-like metadata is mostly non-operative, and one of its freshness signals is broken
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:29:### Finding 4: Modus has summarization helpers, but no actual end-of-session or resume-summary pipeline wires them in
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:36:### Finding 5: Modus’s strongest “context survival across sessions” idea is the learnings subsystem, but it is dormant in the shipped binary
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:43:### Finding 6: Multi-client continuity in Modus is shared-file persistence, not isolated session continuity
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:8:### Finding 1: **Modus’s strongest pattern is a durable proposal inbox implemented as first-class markdown artifacts**
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:15:### Finding 2: **The PR lifecycle is decision-tracking plus belief feedback, not target mutation execution**
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:22:### Finding 3: **The trust-stage model is real state, but in current code it is governance metadata, not enforced runtime gating**
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:29:### Finding 4: **This proposal workflow is not part of Modus’s default memory-only surface; it lives in the broader vault control plane**
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:36:### Finding 5: **Public has auditability and advisory signals, but still lacks the durable triage object that makes Modus’s pattern useful**
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:8:### Finding 1: Modus is **not** a vector or true hybrid retriever; it is a BM25-only engine with optional LLM query rewrites
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:15:### Finding 2: Modus expands queries, but merges variant results with **first-seen dedup**, not global re-ranking
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:22:### Finding 3: The cache idea is useful, but the shipped Jaccard/LRU implementation is unsafe and only partially correct
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:29:### Finding 4: README latency claims only fit the pure in-memory BM25 path; the default librarian-expanded path can be network/LLM-bound
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:36:### Finding 5: Modus’s fact lane is **not BM25**, and it couples search with automatic write-side reinforcement
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:43:### Finding 6: Cross-reference hints are the best transferable search-side idea, but only as a lightweight appendix, not as a ranking core
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:8:### Finding 1: **Modus treats successful recall as a default write-on-read event**
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:15:### Finding 2: **Modus exposes decay, reinforcement, and archival as an operator-facing memory hygiene loop**
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:22:### Finding 3: **The visible “stale” surface in Modus is useful, but its actual scheduling heuristic is much cruder than it looks**
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:29:### Finding 4: **Public already has richer decay/governance primitives than Modus, but they are intentionally hidden behind safer defaults**
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:36:### Finding 5: **The best transfer is a thin review/due-items layer on top of Public’s existing systems—not Modus’s exact fact model**
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:191:### Finding N: [Title]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:7916:### Finding 1: Modus is a single-binary, vault-first MCP server with a deliberately narrow runtime shape
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:7923:### Finding 2: The main search architecture is “scan markdown -> build in-memory services -> serve from RAM”
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:7930:### Finding 3: Modus splits “all documents” from “memory facts” and gives facts their own recall-and-decay loop
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:7937:### Finding 4: Cross-referencing is intentionally shallow adjacency, not a real graph engine
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:7944:### Finding 5: The markdown layer is very portable, but intentionally loose and only lightly normalized
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:7951:### Finding 6: The Librarian is not just documentation rhetoric; it is a real local model dependency in the search architecture
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:7958:### Finding 7: Public already has the broader retrieval architecture; Modus is a simplification reference, not a replacement candidate
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:8011:### Finding 1: Modus is a single-binary, vault-first MCP server with a deliberately narrow runtime shape
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:8018:### Finding 2: The main search architecture is “scan markdown -> build in-memory services -> serve from RAM”
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:8025:### Finding 3: Modus splits “all documents” from “memory facts” and gives facts their own recall-and-decay loop
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:8032:### Finding 4: Cross-referencing is intentionally shallow adjacency, not a real graph engine
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:8039:### Finding 5: The markdown layer is very portable, but intentionally loose and only lightly normalized
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:8046:### Finding 6: The Librarian is not just documentation rhetoric; it is a real local model dependency in the search architecture
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:8053:### Finding 7: Public already has the broader retrieval architecture; Modus is a simplification reference, not a replacement candidate
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:8:### Finding 1: Modus has a **prompt-ready operational learnings plane**, not just retrieval feedback
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:15:### Finding 2: Modus has a **promotion and retirement lifecycle for lessons**, which Public does not visibly expose
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:22:### Finding 3: Modus has an explicit **trust-stage autonomy control plane**
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:29:### Finding 4: Modus models **belief change as proposals with operator adjudication**
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:36:### Finding 5: Modus couples decay to a **stale-memory quarantine workflow**
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:43:### Finding 6: Modus has a lightweight **entity/belief atlas** on top of markdown, not just generic memories
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:8:### Finding 1: Modus has no true fact upsert; it appends filename variants and hides duplicates at read time
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:15:### Finding 2: Modus mutations do not refresh the live in-memory fact store, so hygiene is stale until restart
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:22:### Finding 3: Modus archive is only a partial soft-delete and its visibility rules are inconsistent
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:29:### Finding 4: Topic-key stability is weak because Modus uses raw strings, not canonical alias-backed identities
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:36:### Finding 5: `vault_write` is a destructive overwrite path with no revision trail, checkpoint, or append-only semantics
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:43:### Finding 6: Newly stored facts begin with `created: "now"`, which breaks Modus’s own recency and decay heuristics
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:8:### Finding 1: Modus has no durable search index; it rebuilds the entire retrieval model from markdown on startup
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:15:### Finding 2: The authoritative schema is loose YAML frontmatter plus body; indexed structs are only lossy projections
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:22:### Finding 3: Modus has an immediate consistency split between file writes and searchable state
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:29:### Finding 4: FSRS state is persisted directly in markdown fact files, but the active fact-search model ignores most of it
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:36:### Finding 5: Identity is path-derived, not stable, so renames are semantic changes
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:43:### Finding 6: Public already has the richer persisted storage graph; Modus is a portability reference, not a schema upgrade
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8:### Finding 1: Modus exposes a very thin MCP transport: stdio JSON-RPC with only `initialize`, `tools/list`, and `tools/call`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:15:### Finding 2: Modus has hidden profile separation in code, but the shipped binary hard-wires a single 11-tool profile
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:22:### Finding 3: The only real API endpoints are outbound sidecar calls to the local Librarian model, not Modus-owned service endpoints
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:29:### Finding 4: Tool registration and argument validation are loose enough that adopting Modus’s MCP schema pattern would weaken Public
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:36:### Finding 5: Modus search tools hide side effects and capability changes behind ambient conditions rather than explicit flags
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:43:### Finding 6: Modus’s CLI is a real second operator surface, separate from MCP, and that split is one of the cleaner parts of the design
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:8:### Finding 1: **Modus treats memory as a multi-plane operating system, not one indexed corpus**
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:15:### Finding 2: **Governance should include an explicit autonomy stage, not just access control**
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:22:### Finding 3: **High-risk memory changes should pass through a durable proposal inbox**
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:29:### Finding 4: **Decay should drive visible workflow states, not stay buried inside scoring and cleanup**
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:36:### Finding 5: **Memory can become an execution-readiness layer, not just a recall layer**
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:8:### Finding 1: Modus’s BM25 stack is clean, but Public’s lexical lane is already stronger and sits inside a broader retrieval system
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:15:### Finding 2: Modus’s fuzzy query cache is one real retrieval idea Public does not already have
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:22:### Finding 3: Modus’s “Librarian expansion” is a local LLM rewrite path, while Public already has three expansion families
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:29:### Finding 4: Modus’s best retrieval-specific pattern is not its graph model, but its explicit “connected docs not in top hits” appendix
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:36:### Finding 5: Modus’s fact-only retrieval lane is fast, but too narrow to replace Public’s scoped hybrid memory search
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:43:### Finding 6: Modus’s retrieval README overstates the hot path; only the cache story is source-plausible
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:7:### Finding 1: **Reject Modus’s fuzzy result-cache architecture; it is the wrong cache contract for Public**
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:14:### Finding 2: **Do not simplify deep-mode query expansion to Modus’s “union then cap” flow**
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:21:### Finding 3: **Adopt a stricter “one retrieval core” rule; Modus’s duplicated fact search paths are a warning**
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:28:### Finding 4: **Refactor Public’s access-writeback out of Stage 2 fusion before expanding it further**
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:35:### Finding 5: **If we want Modus-style “connected docs” ergonomics, add them as a formatter over existing graph evidence, not as a new index**
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:8:### Finding 1: Modus’s “single Librarian / sole writer” model is documentation-only; the shipped MCP server does not enforce it
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:15:### Finding 2: In Modus, search is a write-side effect, so one agent’s recall mutates shared memory for every other agent
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:22:### Finding 3: Modus’s only real project boundary is the vault root; inside a vault, everything is global
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:29:### Finding 4: Passive capture is mostly aspirational in source; the shipped server depends on explicit memory tool use
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:36:### Finding 5: Modus’s dormant `learnings` subsystem is the strongest agent-agnostic idea in this repo
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:43:### Finding 6: Modus has a good optional-sidecar shape for agent integration, but the implementation is hard-wired and the README overstates what ships
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:7:### Finding 1: Modus boots a vault process, not a resumable session lifecycle
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:14:### Finding 2: Modus’s only session field is decorative, not an enforced partition
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:21:### Finding 3: Public’s trusted session lifecycle is a real control plane; Modus has no equivalent
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:28:### Finding 4: Public’s resume/bootstrap path is compositional and fail-closed; Modus only offers raw vault health
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:35:### Finding 5: Modus’s write lifecycle is immediate markdown mutation, but it is not same-session freshness-safe
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:42:### Finding 6: Modus treats recall as an implicit global write; Public’s two-timescale lifecycle is safer
