import path from "node:path";
import { type Plugin, tool } from "@opencode-ai/plugin";

export const MnemosynePlugin: Plugin = async (ctx) => {
  const { directory, worktree, client } = ctx;
  const targetDir = directory || worktree || process.cwd();

  const log = {
    debug: (msg: string) =>
      client.app
        .log({ body: { service: "mnemosyne", level: "debug", message: msg } })
        .catch(() => {}),
    info: (msg: string) =>
      client.app
        .log({ body: { service: "mnemosyne", level: "info", message: msg } })
        .catch(() => {}),
    warn: (msg: string) =>
      client.app
        .log({ body: { service: "mnemosyne", level: "warn", message: msg } })
        .catch(() => {}),
    error: (msg: string) =>
      client.app
        .log({ body: { service: "mnemosyne", level: "error", message: msg } })
        .catch(() => {}),
  };

  // Strip trailing slashes but keep the root slash if it's just "/"
  let projectDir = targetDir.replace(/(.+?)\/+$/, "$1");
  const projectRaw = path.basename(projectDir);
  const project = projectRaw === "global" ? "default" : (projectRaw || "default");

  await log.debug(`Plugin loaded for project: ${project} (dir: ${targetDir})`);

  /**
   * Run the mnemosyne CLI binary gracefully using Bun.spawn.
   * Avoids shell interpolation entirely by passing args as array.
   */
  async function mnemosyne(...args: string[]): Promise<string> {
    await log.debug(`Executing: mnemosyne ${args.join(" ")}`);
    try {
      // @ts-ignore - Bun is globally available in opencode environment
      const proc = Bun.spawn(["mnemosyne", ...args], {
        cwd: targetDir,
        stdout: "pipe",
        stderr: "pipe",
      });

      const [stdout, stderr, exitCode] = await Promise.all([
        new Response(proc.stdout).text(),
        new Response(proc.stderr).text(),
        proc.exited,
      ]);

      if (exitCode !== 0) {
        await log.error(`Execution failed (code ${exitCode}): ${stderr}`);
        throw new Error(stderr.trim() || `mnemosyne ${args[0]} failed`);
      }

      // mnemosyne may write output to stderr (older versions), use whichever has content
      const output = stdout || stderr;
      await log.debug(`Execution successful. Output size: ${output.length}`);
      return output;
    }
    catch (e: unknown) {
      await log.error(`Execution error: ${e instanceof Error ? e.stack : String(e)}`);
      const msg = e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e);
      if (
        msg.includes("not found") ||
        msg.includes("ENOENT") ||
        msg.includes("No such file")
      ) {
        return "Error: mnemosyne binary not found. Install it: https://github.com/gandazgul/mnemosyne#install";
      }
      throw e;
    }
  }

  // Auto-init the project collection (idempotent).
  try {
    // @ts-ignore
    await Bun.spawn(["mnemosyne", "init", "--name", project], {
      cwd: targetDir,
      stdout: "ignore", // Silence "collection already exists" logs
      stderr: "pipe",   // Keep stderr for critical errors
    }).exited;
    await log.info(`Ensured collection exists: ${project}`);
  }
  catch (e) {
    await log.warn(`Failed to auto-init collection: ${e}`);
  }

  return {
    // ── Tools ──────────────────────────────────────────────

    tool: {
      memory_recall: tool({
        description:
          "Search project memory for relevant context, past decisions, and preferences. Use this at the start of conversations and whenever past context would help.",
        args: {
          query: tool.schema.string().describe("Semantic search query"),
        },
        async execute(args) {
          await log.info(`Searching project memory for: ${args.query}`);
          // Quote the query to prevent SQLite FTS errors with hyphens and special characters
          const safeQuery = `"${args.query.replaceAll('"', '""')}"`;
          const result = await mnemosyne(
            "search",
            "--name",
            project,
            "--format",
            "plain",
            safeQuery,
          );
          return result.trim() || "No memories found.";
        },
      }),

      memory_recall_global: tool({
        description:
          "Search global memory for cross-project preferences, decisions and patterns.",
        args: {
          query: tool.schema.string().describe("Semantic search query"),
        },
        async execute(args) {
          await log.info(`Searching global memory for: ${args.query}`);
          const safeQuery = `"${args.query.replaceAll('"', '""')}"`;
          const result = await mnemosyne(
            "search",
            "--global",
            "--format",
            "plain",
            safeQuery,
          );
          return result.trim() || "No global memories found.";
        },
      }),

      memory_store: tool({
        description:
          "Store a project memory: a decision, preference, or important context. One concise concept per memory. Set core=true for critical context that should always be available in every session (use sparingly).",
        args: {
          content: tool.schema.string().describe("Concise memory to store"),
          core: tool.schema.boolean().optional().describe(
            "If true, this memory is always injected into context (like AGENTS.md). Use sparingly."
          ),
        },
        async execute(args) {
          await log.info(`Storing project memory: ${args.content}`);
          const cmdArgs = ["add", "--name", project];
          if (args.core) {
            cmdArgs.push("--tag", "core");
          }
          cmdArgs.push(args.content);
          return (
            await mnemosyne(...cmdArgs)
          ).trim();
        },
      }),

      memory_store_global: tool({
        description:
          "Store a cross-project memory: personal preferences, coding style, tool choices. Set core=true for critical cross-project context that should always be available.",
        args: {
          content: tool.schema.string().describe("Global memory to store"),
          core: tool.schema.boolean().optional().describe(
            "If true, this memory is always injected into context. Use sparingly."
          ),
        },
        async execute(args) {
          await log.info(`Storing global memory: ${args.content}`);
          // Ensure the global collection exists.
          try {
            // @ts-ignore
            await Bun.spawn(["mnemosyne", "init", "--global"], {
              cwd: targetDir,
              stdout: "ignore", // Silence "collection already exists" logs
              stderr: "pipe",   // Keep stderr for critical errors
            }).exited;
            await log.info("Ensured global collection exists.");
          }
          catch (e) {
            await log.warn(`Failed to auto-init global collection: ${e}`);
          }
          const cmdArgs = ["add", "--global"];
          if (args.core) {
            cmdArgs.push("--tag", "core");
          }
          cmdArgs.push(args.content);
          return (await mnemosyne(...cmdArgs)).trim();
        },
      }),

      memory_delete: tool({
        description:
          "Delete an outdated or incorrect memory by its document ID (shown in [brackets] in recall/list results).",
        args: {
          id: tool.schema.number().describe("Document ID to delete"),
        },
        async execute(args) {
          await log.info(`Deleting memory document ID: ${args.id}`);
          return (await mnemosyne("delete", String(args.id))).trim();
        },
      }),
    },

    // ── Hooks ──────────────────────────────────────────────

    // Inject memory instructions into compaction so they survive
    // context window resets.
    "experimental.session.compacting": async (_input, output) => {
      output.context.push(`## Persistent Memory (mnemosyne)

You have persistent memory tools: memory_recall, memory_store, memory_delete,
memory_recall_global, memory_store_global.

When to use memory:
- Search memory when past context would help answer the user's request.
- Store concise summaries of important decisions, preferences, and patterns.
- Delete outdated memories when new decisions contradict them.
- Use **core** for facts that should always be in context (project architecture, key conventions, user preferences).
- Use **global** variants for cross-project preferences (coding style, tool choices).
- At the end of a session, store any relevant memories for future sessions.`);
    },
  };
};

---PACKAGE---

  "name": "opencode-mnemosyne",
  "version": "0.2.4",
  "description": "OpenCode plugin for local persistent memory using Mnemosyne — offline semantic search, no cloud required",

  "main": "dist/index.js",
  "types": "dist/index.d.ts",
  "scripts": {
    "build": "tsc",
    "dev": "tsc --watch",
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit",
    "ci": "tsc --noEmit && tsc"
  },

  },
  "opencode": {
    "type": "plugin"
  },
  "files": [
    "dist"
  ]
}

---README---
# opencode-mnemosyne

OpenCode plugin for **local persistent memory** using [Mnemosyne](https://github.com/gandazgul/mnemosyne). Gives your AI coding agent memory that persists across sessions -- entirely offline, no cloud APIs.

This is the local/offline alternative to cloud-based memory plugins like opencode-supermemory.

## Prerequisites

Install the mnemosyne binary first:

```bash
# From source (requires Go 1.21+, GCC, Task)
git clone https://github.com/gandazgul/mnemosyne.git
cd mnemosyne
task install
```

See the [mnemosyne README](https://github.com/gandazgul/mnemosyne#quick-start) for detailed setup instructions. On first use, mnemosyne will automatically download its ML models (~500 MB one-time).

## Install

Add to your `opencode.json`:

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://opencode.ai/config.json",
  "plugin": ["opencode-mnemosyne"]
}
```

That's it. OpenCode will install the plugin automatically.

## What it does

### Tools

The plugin registers five tools available to the AI agent:

| Tool | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `memory_recall` | Search project memory for relevant context and past decisions |
| `memory_recall_global` | Search global memory for cross-project preferences |
| `memory_store` | Store a project-scoped memory (optionally as `core`) |
| `memory_store_global` | Store a cross-project memory (optionally as `core`) |
| `memory_delete` | Delete an outdated memory by its document ID |

### Hooks

- **`experimental.session.compacting`** -- Injects memory tool instructions into the compaction prompt so the agent retains awareness of its memory capabilities across context window resets.

### Memory scoping

| Scope | Collection | Persists across |
|-------|-----------|-----------------|
| Project | `<directory-name>` | Sessions in the same project |
| Global | `global` | All projects |
| Core (project) | `<directory-name>` (tagged `core`) | Sessions + survives compaction |
| Core (global) | `global` (tagged `core`) | All projects + survives compaction |

The project collection is auto-initialized when the plugin loads. The global collection is created on first use of `memory_store_global`.

## AGENTS.md (recommended)

For best results, add this to your project or global `AGENTS.md` so the agent uses memory proactively from the start of each session:

```markdown
## Memory (mnemosyne)

- At the start of a session, use memory_recall and memory_recall_global to search for context
  relevant to the user's first message.
- After significant decisions, use memory_store to save a concise summary.
- Delete contradicted memories with memory_delete before storing updated ones.
- Use memory_recall_global / memory_store_global for cross-project preferences.
- Mark critical, always-relevant context as core (core=true) — but use sparingly.
- When you are done with a session, store any memories that you think are relevant
  to the user and the project. This will help you recall important information in
  future sessions.
```

## How it works

Mnemosyne is a local document store with hybrid search:
- **Full-text search** (SQLite FTS5, BM25 ranking)
- **Vector search** (sqlite-vec, cosine similarity with snowflake-arctic-embed-m-v1.5)
- **Reciprocal Rank Fusion** combines both for best results

All ML inference runs locally via ONNX Runtime. Your memories never leave your machine.

## Development

This project uses standard Node.js tools: `npm` for package management and `tsc` (TypeScript compiler) for building.

```bash
# Install dependencies
npm install

# Build the project
npm run build

# Start the compiler in watch mode for development
npm run dev

# Run TypeScript checks
npm run typecheck
```

## License

MIT

 succeeded in 0ms:
### Finding 1: Engram is built as a store-centered hub with thin delivery surfaces
- **Source**: [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/cmd/engram/main.go#L113](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/cmd/engram/main.go#L113); [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md#L119](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md#L119)
- **What it does**: `cmd/engram/main.go` dispatches every surface from one binary: `serve`, `mcp`, `tui`, `search`, `save`, `context`, `sync`, `setup`. The architecture doc matches that by making `internal/store/store.go` the core, with MCP, HTTP, sync, setup, and TUI as adapters around it.
- **Why it matters for us**: Public already has richer retrieval than Engram, but its memory system spans many MCP tools, scripts, hooks, and supporting subsystems. Engram’s clearer “one core store, many thin surfaces” boundary is a useful architecture lens for reducing wrapper drift.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Impact**: medium

### Finding 2: Engram explicitly separates agent-safe MCP tools from admin/curation tools
- **Source**: [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/mcp/mcp.go#L50](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/mcp/mcp.go#L50); [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/mcp/mcp.go#L124](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/mcp/mcp.go#L124); [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts#L878](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts#L878); [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L851](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L851)
- **What it does**: Engram defines `ProfileAgent` and `ProfileAdmin`, resolves them through `ResolveTools()`, and pairs that with “core” versus “deferred” tool instructions. Public currently exposes a flat `TOOL_DEFINITIONS` list from the server without an equivalent profile gate.
- **Why it matters for us**: This is a concrete answer to the “tool clutter” problem. Public has a much broader MCP surface, so profile-aware listing or wrapper-level profiles could reduce prompt noise and lower misuse risk without removing power-user tools.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 3: Engram treats sessions, prompts, and summaries as first-class persisted objects
- **Source**: [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L441](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L441); [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L754](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L754); [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L1613](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L1613); [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/mcp/mcp.go#L460](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/mcp/mcp.go#L460); [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-resume.ts#L400](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-resume.ts#L400); [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts#L163](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts#L163)
- **What it does**: Engram persists `sessions`, `observations`, and `user_prompts` as separate tables, exposes `mem_session_start`, `mem_session_end`, and `mem_session_summary`, then composes startup context by formatting recent sessions, prompts, and observations together. Public has strong resume/bootstrap orchestration, but it is centered on recovery composition rather than a lightweight explicit session-start/session-end API.
- **Why it matters for us**: Public is already stronger at startup recovery and structural readiness, but Engram’s explicit lifecycle tools would add a simpler durable narrative boundary around a session. That is especially useful for compaction survival and for cleaner prompt/session/history analytics.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 4: Engram’s memory hygiene revolves around stable topic threads plus duplicate suppression
- **Source**: [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L948](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L948); [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L3198](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L3198); [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md#L86](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md#L86); [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/memory/generate-context.ts#L75](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/memory/generate-context.ts#L75); [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-save.ts#L1078](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-save.ts#L1078)
- **What it does**: `AddObservation()` first upserts on `topic_key + project + scope`, incrementing `revision_count`; if no topic match exists, it checks a rolling duplicate window and increments `duplicate_count` instead of inserting a new row. Public’s save path is file-first and quality-gated through `generate-context.ts` and `memory_save`, but it does not expose a comparable first-class thread-key convention for evolving topics.
- **Why it matters for us**: This is one of Engram’s strongest durable ideas. Public already preserves richer markdown memory artifacts, but long-running decisions and repeated refinements would benefit from an optional stable thread key so related saves revise one conceptual thread instead of fragmenting across many files.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 5: Engram’s search is intentionally simple: direct topic-key shortcut first, sanitized FTS5 second
- **Source**: [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L1462](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L1462); [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L3382](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L3382); [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L482](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L482); [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-context.ts#L1078](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-context.ts#L1078)
- **What it does**: If the query contains `/`, Engram tries direct `topic_key` lookup first and assigns those hits a dominant rank, then falls back to quoted FTS5 `MATCH` ordered by `fts.rank`. Public’s `memory_search` is much broader: vector + lexical + rerank + intent routing + session/causal boosts, while `memory_context` adds orchestration and graph-aware routing.
- **Why it matters for us**: Engram’s lexical path is not better than Public’s hybrid stack overall, but its exact-thread shortcut is useful. The idea to copy is the deterministic “if this looks like a thread key, bypass normal ranking,” not the FTS-only architecture.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Impact**: medium

### Finding 6: Agent-agnostic adoption is driven by setup/install code and hook-level protocol injection, not by core-store complexity
- **Source**: [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/setup/setup.go#L1](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/setup/setup.go#L1); [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/plugin/opencode/engram.ts#L1](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/plugin/opencode/engram.ts#L1); [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/plugin/claude-code/scripts/session-start.sh#L1](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/plugin/claude-code/scripts/session-start.sh#L1); [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/README.md#L45](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/README.md#L45)
- **What it does**: `setup.go` writes/install-configures agent-specific MCP integrations for OpenCode, Claude Code, Gemini CLI, and Codex. The OpenCode adapter is explicitly “thin,” and the Claude session-start hook starts the server, creates a session, imports sync state, fetches context, and injects protocol text on startup.
- **Why it matters for us**: The strong pattern here is distribution discipline: keep setup friction low, keep hooks small, and keep behavioral protocol near the runtime seam. Public already supports more advanced runtime recovery, but Engram’s packaging is cleaner and easier to reason about.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Impact**: medium

## Sources Consulted

---
### Finding 1: Mex’s real innovation is a separate knowledge control plane, not markdown storage alone
- **Source**: [external/README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/README.md#L32), [external/ROUTER.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/ROUTER.md#L56), [external/src/cli.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/cli.ts#L28), [external/src/drift/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/index.ts#L17), [external/src/sync/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/index.ts#L29)
- **What it does**: Mex explicitly splits the system into `init` pre-analysis, `check` static drift detection, `sync` targeted repair, and `pattern add` scaffold growth. `ROUTER.md` then turns that into an operating loop: `CONTEXT -> BUILD -> VERIFY -> DEBUG -> GROW`. The important part is that markdown is not trusted by default; it is continuously audited and selectively repaired.
- **Why it matters for us**: Spec Kit Memory is strong at retrieval, resume, trigger recall, and save-time quality, but it does not yet have a dedicated repo-wide “is our durable project truth still true?” plane. Mex adds that missing authority surface.
- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 2: The hinge is claim extraction: Mex converts prose into typed, checkable assertions
- **Source**: [external/src/drift/claims.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/claims.ts#L36), [external/src/drift/checkers/edges.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/edges.ts#L5), [external/src/drift/checkers/command.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/command.ts#L5), [external/src/drift/checkers/dependency.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/dependency.ts#L38), [external/src/drift/checkers/cross-file.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/cross-file.ts#L3), [external/src/drift/checkers/index-sync.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/index-sync.ts#L6)
- **What it does**: `extractClaims()` mines inline code, code blocks, bold dependency names, and version phrases into typed claims: `path`, `command`, `dependency`, and `version`. It deliberately skips template placeholders, HTTP routes, code snippets, and negated sections. The checker suite then validates each claim family against disk, frontmatter edges, manifests, and other scaffold files.
- **Why it matters for us**: Our current guardrails validate memory file quality after write and recover session context well, but they do not parse repo guidance and durable docs into machine-auditable claims. That is the core mechanism we would need if we want automated doc-to-code truth checks rather than just structure checks.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 3: Targeted sync is the strongest adoptable pattern because it repairs only the broken knowledge surface
- **Source**: [external/src/sync/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/index.ts#L47), [external/src/sync/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/index.ts#L61), [external/src/sync/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/index.ts#L144), [external/src/sync/brief-builder.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/brief-builder.ts#L41), [external/src/sync/brief-builder.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/brief-builder.ts#L58), [external/src/sync/brief-builder.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/brief-builder.ts#L99), [external/src/git.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/git.ts#L52), [external/SYNC.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/SYNC.md#L44)
- **What it does**: `runSync()` reruns drift detection, filters to error-bearing files by default, groups issues per file, and builds a combined brief. That brief includes the exact issue list, current file content, nearby filesystem context for missing paths, and a recent git diff for referenced paths. The repair instruction is explicitly bounded: change only what is necessary, then re-run drift and loop again.
- **Why it matters for us**: This is the clearest gap in Spec Kit Memory. We have `memory_health`, `session_resume`, `session_bootstrap`, `post-save-review`, and `validate-memory-quality`, but none of them emits a minimal, evidence-rich remediation packet for stale repo guidance, spec docs, or memory-facing documentation. Mex’s repair loop is the most directly portable idea.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 4: Mex’s freshness model is useful because it measures both elapsed time and repo churn
- **Source**: [external/src/drift/checkers/staleness.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/staleness.ts#L4), [external/src/drift/checkers/staleness.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/staleness.ts#L17), [external/src/git.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/git.ts#L12), [external/src/git.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/git.ts#L31), [session-resume.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-resume.ts#L400), [session-bootstrap.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts#L163)
- **What it does**: Every scaffold file gets two staleness checks: days since last change and commits since last change, with thresholds of `30/90` days and `50/200` commits. That means a doc can be stale because the repo changed around it rapidly even if not much wall-clock time passed.
- **Why it matters for us**: Our freshness signals today are mostly runtime recovery signals: session continuity, code-graph readiness, CocoIndex availability, database health. Mex suggests a new axis: “durable project truth freshness relative to repo activity.” That complements, rather than replaces, `session_resume` and `session_bootstrap`.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Impact**: medium

### Finding 5: Mex also checks omissions, not just false statements, via reality-to-scaffold coverage
- **Source**: [external/src/drift/checkers/script-coverage.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/script-coverage.ts#L26), [external/README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/README.md#L72), [memory-crud-health.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts#L222), [post-save-review.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/core/post-save-review.ts#L8), [validate-memory-quality.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/validate-memory-quality.ts#L45)
- **What it does**: `checkScriptCoverage()` loads `package.json` scripts, ignores lifecycle noise, and warns when a real script is not mentioned anywhere in scaffold text. That is a reverse audit: reality proves docs incomplete, even when docs never made a wrong claim.
- **Why it matters for us**: Spec Kit’s current checks are stronger on write-time artifact integrity and runtime system health than on omission detection in durable repo guidance. A lightweight “undocumented operational surface” checker would add value, especially for scripts, commands, and perhaps major MCP surfaces. The caveat is that Mex’s implementation is intentionally shallow string matching, so we should copy the pattern, not the exact heuristic.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Impact**: medium

## Assessment
- New information ratio: 0.74
- Questions addressed: strongest end-to-end pattern; how Mex turns markdown into auditable claims; whether targeted sync is meaningfully different from our current health/save surfaces; whether repo-activity freshness is a distinct signal; whether omission detection adds value beyond structural validation.
- Questions answered: the strongest pattern is a separate doc-truth control plane; targeted sync is the highest-value missing capability for us; claim extraction is the technical hinge that makes the plane possible; dual-signal freshness is worth adding as a new axis; omission checks are useful, but Mex’s exact heuristics should be treated as prototypes, not copied wholesale.

## Reflection
- What worked: tracing the loop in execution order, `ROUTER.md` contract -> `cli.ts` surfaces -> `claims.ts` extraction -> checker family -> `sync/brief-builder.ts` repair packet -> recheck, made the actual innovation much clearer than reading checker summaries in isolation.
- What did not work: the prompt’s `external/mex-main` path is stale in this checkout, the real snapshot is `external/`; the strict validator passed, but it still emitted `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`, so the pass signal was clean but noisy.

## Recommended Next Focus
Compare Modus Memory and Mnemosyne against this same control-plane question: do either of them provide a durable truth-audit surface with bounded remediation, or are they still primarily storage and retrieval systems? The sharpest next cut is provenance and trust: which system, if any, has a stronger answer than Mex for evidence-backed reusable guidance without placeholder-first drift.

# Iteration 014: DEEP DIVE - STRONGEST PATTERN

## Focus
DEEP DIVE - STRONGEST PATTERN: Go deep on the single most impactful pattern from this system. Trace it end-to-end.

## Findings
### Finding 1: Mex’s real innovation is a separate knowledge control plane, not markdown storage alone
- **Source**: [external/README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/README.md#L32), [external/ROUTER.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/ROUTER.md#L56), [external/src/cli.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/cli.ts#L28), [external/src/drift/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/index.ts#L17), [external/src/sync/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/index.ts#L29)
- **What it does**: Mex explicitly splits the system into `init` pre-analysis, `check` static drift detection, `sync` targeted repair, and `pattern add` scaffold growth. `ROUTER.md` then turns that into an operating loop: `CONTEXT -> BUILD -> VERIFY -> DEBUG -> GROW`. The important part is that markdown is not trusted by default; it is continuously audited and selectively repaired.
- **Why it matters for us**: Spec Kit Memory is strong at retrieval, resume, trigger recall, and save-time quality, but it does not yet have a dedicated repo-wide “is our durable project truth still true?” plane. Mex adds that missing authority surface.
- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 2: The hinge is claim extraction: Mex converts prose into typed, checkable assertions
- **Source**: [external/src/drift/claims.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/claims.ts#L36), [external/src/drift/checkers/edges.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/edges.ts#L5), [external/src/drift/checkers/command.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/command.ts#L5), [external/src/drift/checkers/dependency.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/dependency.ts#L38), [external/src/drift/checkers/cross-file.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/cross-file.ts#L3), [external/src/drift/checkers/index-sync.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/index-sync.ts#L6)
- **What it does**: `extractClaims()` mines inline code, code blocks, bold dependency names, and version phrases into typed claims: `path`, `command`, `dependency`, and `version`. It deliberately skips template placeholders, HTTP routes, code snippets, and negated sections. The checker suite then validates each claim family against disk, frontmatter edges, manifests, and other scaffold files.
- **Why it matters for us**: Our current guardrails validate memory file quality after write and recover session context well, but they do not parse repo guidance and durable docs into machine-auditable claims. That is the core mechanism we would need if we want automated doc-to-code truth checks rather than just structure checks.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 3: Targeted sync is the strongest adoptable pattern because it repairs only the broken knowledge surface
- **Source**: [external/src/sync/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/index.ts#L47), [external/src/sync/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/index.ts#L61), [external/src/sync/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/index.ts#L144), [external/src/sync/brief-builder.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/brief-builder.ts#L41), [external/src/sync/brief-builder.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/brief-builder.ts#L58), [external/src/sync/brief-builder.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/brief-builder.ts#L99), [external/src/git.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/git.ts#L52), [external/SYNC.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/SYNC.md#L44)
- **What it does**: `runSync()` reruns drift detection, filters to error-bearing files by default, groups issues per file, and builds a combined brief. That brief includes the exact issue list, current file content, nearby filesystem context for missing paths, and a recent git diff for referenced paths. The repair instruction is explicitly bounded: change only what is necessary, then re-run drift and loop again.
- **Why it matters for us**: This is the clearest gap in Spec Kit Memory. We have `memory_health`, `session_resume`, `session_bootstrap`, `post-save-review`, and `validate-memory-quality`, but none of them emits a minimal, evidence-rich remediation packet for stale repo guidance, spec docs, or memory-facing documentation. Mex’s repair loop is the most directly portable idea.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 4: Mex’s freshness model is useful because it measures both elapsed time and repo churn
- **Source**: [external/src/drift/checkers/staleness.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/staleness.ts#L4), [external/src/drift/checkers/staleness.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/staleness.ts#L17), [external/src/git.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/git.ts#L12), [external/src/git.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/git.ts#L31), [session-resume.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-resume.ts#L400), [session-bootstrap.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts#L163)
- **What it does**: Every scaffold file gets two staleness checks: days since last change and commits since last change, with thresholds of `30/90` days and `50/200` commits. That means a doc can be stale because the repo changed around it rapidly even if not much wall-clock time passed.
- **Why it matters for us**: Our freshness signals today are mostly runtime recovery signals: session continuity, code-graph readiness, CocoIndex availability, database health. Mex suggests a new axis: “durable project truth freshness relative to repo activity.” That complements, rather than replaces, `session_resume` and `session_bootstrap`.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Impact**: medium

### Finding 5: Mex also checks omissions, not just false statements, via reality-to-scaffold coverage
- **Source**: [external/src/drift/checkers/script-coverage.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/script-coverage.ts#L26), [external/README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/README.md#L72), [memory-crud-health.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts#L222), [post-save-review.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/core/post-save-review.ts#L8), [validate-memory-quality.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/validate-memory-quality.ts#L45)
- **What it does**: `checkScriptCoverage()` loads `package.json` scripts, ignores lifecycle noise, and warns when a real script is not mentioned anywhere in scaffold text. That is a reverse audit: reality proves docs incomplete, even when docs never made a wrong claim.
- **Why it matters for us**: Spec Kit’s current checks are stronger on write-time artifact integrity and runtime system health than on omission detection in durable repo guidance. A lightweight “undocumented operational surface” checker would add value, especially for scripts, commands, and perhaps major MCP surfaces. The caveat is that Mex’s implementation is intentionally shallow string matching, so we should copy the pattern, not the exact heuristic.

---
### Finding 1: MemPalace uses `status` as an agent-behavior bootstrap, while Public spreads the same job across richer recovery surfaces
- **Source**: [mcp_server.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/mcp_server.py#L139), [mcp_server.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/mcp_server.py#L169), [session-bootstrap.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts#L163), [session-resume.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-resume.ts#L409), [context-server.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L755) `source-confirmed`
- **What it does**: `mempalace_status` returns counts plus an embedded `PALACE_PROTOCOL` and `AAAK_SPEC`, explicitly telling the agent to query before speaking, write a diary after each session, and invalidate changed facts. Public distributes this behavior across `session_bootstrap()`, `session_resume()`, dynamic server instructions, and structural routing nudges instead of one memory-facing bootstrap tool.
- **Why it matters for us**: MemPalace is weaker on recovery depth, but stronger on “one call teaches the agent how to behave.” Public already has the better lifecycle substrate; the reusable idea is a concise behavior contract on the canonical recovery surface, not a new backend.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 2: MemPalace’s wake-up stack is mostly prompt packaging; Public’s resume path is materially richer state management
- **Source**: [layers.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/layers.py#L34), [layers.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/layers.py#L76), [layers.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/layers.py#L185), [layers.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/layers.py#L369), [memory-context.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-context.ts#L783), [memory-context.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-context.ts#L900), [session-resume.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-resume.ts#L471) `source-confirmed`
- **What it does**: MemPalace `wake_up()` is `L0 identity.txt + L1 top 15 drawers`, with `L2` doing plain `col.get()` metadata filtering and `L3` doing the same Chroma query as normal search. Public’s resume path uses anchor-scoped `memory_search`, auto-resume heuristics, structural freshness, CocoIndex availability, and a shared payload contract.
- **Why it matters for us**: The MemPalace stack is a good human-readable startup shape, but not a deeper lifecycle system than what Public already has. The reusable part is the compact “identity + essential story + on-demand deepening” packaging, not the underlying retrieval/state model.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 3: MemPalace’s strongest lifecycle contribution is forced save timing before loss, not better saved-state structure
- **Source**: [hooks/README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/hooks/README.md#L7), [mempal_save_hook.sh](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/hooks/mempal_save_hook.sh#L55), [mempal_precompact_hook.sh](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/hooks/mempal_precompact_hook.sh#L49), [generate-context.js](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js#L85), [spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js#L396) `source-confirmed`
- **What it does**: MemPalace’s Stop hook blocks every `SAVE_INTERVAL` human turns and its PreCompact hook always blocks, emitting a system reason that tells the agent to save before stopping or compaction. Public has a strong explicit save path in `generate-context.js` and compaction-safe transport injection, but no equivalent forced preservation checkpoint before context loss.
- **Why it matters for us**: This is the highest-leverage lifecycle idea in the repo. Public already survives compaction structurally, but MemPalace addresses a different failure mode: the user or agent simply forgetting to persist key session context before it disappears.
- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 4: MemPalace session state is intentionally light; Public already has the stronger crash-recovery and trusted-session model
- **Source**: [mempal_save_hook.sh](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/hooks/mempal_save_hook.sh#L85), [mempal_precompact_hook.sh](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/hooks/mempal_precompact_hook.sh#L57), [memory-context.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-context.ts#L821), [context-server.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L1208), [context-server.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L1789) `source-confirmed`
- **What it does**: MemPalace tracks save cycles with `stop_hook_active`, transcript counts, session IDs, and per-session last-save files under `~/.mempalace/hook_state/`. Public resolves trusted session IDs, preserves session lifecycle metadata, recovers interrupted sessions, recovers pending memory files on startup, and resets incomplete ingest jobs after crashes.
- **Why it matters for us**: Public should not simplify its lifecycle state down to MemPalace’s hook-counter model. If we copy the hook timing idea, it should sit on top of the current trusted session/recovery stack rather than replace it.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 5: MemPalace leaves save-time organization to the agent or heuristics; Public’s structured save contract is more auditable and safer
- **Source**: [miner.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/miner.py#L278), [miner.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/miner.py#L325), [convo_miner.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/convo_miner.py#L290), [general_extractor.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/general_extractor.py#L363), [onboarding.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/onboarding.py#L266), [generate-context.js](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js#L85) `source-confirmed`
- **What it does**: MemPalace either mines/chunks text into drawers with heuristic room assignment or blocks the agent and asks it to organize/save using its own current context. Public’s `generate-context.js` is explicitly JSON-primary, treats caller-provided structured session data as authoritative, and preserves exact target-folder authority plus tool/exchange/preflight/postflight context.
- **Why it matters for us**: For lifecycle correctness, Public’s save path is currently better. MemPalace’s free-form save timing is useful, but its save semantics are less auditable than our structured context handoff.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: high

## Assessment
- New information ratio: 0.76
- Questions addressed: status/bootstrap behavior, wake-up layering, forced-save timing, crash/session state, save-path authority
- Questions answered: MemPalace’s best lifecycle idea is enforced pre-loss saving; Public already exceeds it on recovery depth, state trust, and structured save semantics; the main reusable piece is concise bootstrap guidance, not a retrieval/state reset

## Reflection
- What worked: tracing `mcp_server.py`, `layers.py`, hooks, and our `session-bootstrap.ts`/`session-resume.ts`/`memory-context.ts` together made the lifecycle split very clear: MemPalace is strong on behavioral nudging and timing, Public is strong on authoritative state and recovery contracts
- What did not work: the requested phase prerequisites are not present because `spec.md`, `plan.md`, `tasks.md`, `checklist.md`, and `decision-record.md` are all missing in `005-mempalace`; the strict validator still returned `RESULT: PASSED` while also emitting `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`; and I could not run the exact write-based deep-research/save workflow in this read-only sandbox

## Recommended Next Focus
Compare actor/topic identity next: trace MemPalace’s entity registry, AAAK codes, diary wings, and KG entity IDs against Public’s `tenantId`/`userId`/`agentId`/`sharedSpaceId`, trigger phrases, and causal links to decide whether a human-friendly alias layer should exist only at the UX edge or also participate in governed retrieval.

# Iteration 009: COMPARISON - LIFECYCLE

## Focus
COMPARISON - LIFECYCLE: Compare session/state management against our `memory_context`, `generate-context.js`, `session_bootstrap`.

## Findings
### Finding 1: MemPalace uses `status` as an agent-behavior bootstrap, while Public spreads the same job across richer recovery surfaces
- **Source**: [mcp_server.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/mcp_server.py#L139), [mcp_server.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/mcp_server.py#L169), [session-bootstrap.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts#L163), [session-resume.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-resume.ts#L409), [context-server.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L755) `source-confirmed`
- **What it does**: `mempalace_status` returns counts plus an embedded `PALACE_PROTOCOL` and `AAAK_SPEC`, explicitly telling the agent to query before speaking, write a diary after each session, and invalidate changed facts. Public distributes this behavior across `session_bootstrap()`, `session_resume()`, dynamic server instructions, and structural routing nudges instead of one memory-facing bootstrap tool.
- **Why it matters for us**: MemPalace is weaker on recovery depth, but stronger on “one call teaches the agent how to behave.” Public already has the better lifecycle substrate; the reusable idea is a concise behavior contract on the canonical recovery surface, not a new backend.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 2: MemPalace’s wake-up stack is mostly prompt packaging; Public’s resume path is materially richer state management
- **Source**: [layers.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/layers.py#L34), [layers.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/layers.py#L76), [layers.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/layers.py#L185), [layers.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/layers.py#L369), [memory-context.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-context.ts#L783), [memory-context.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-context.ts#L900), [session-resume.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-resume.ts#L471) `source-confirmed`
- **What it does**: MemPalace `wake_up()` is `L0 identity.txt + L1 top 15 drawers`, with `L2` doing plain `col.get()` metadata filtering and `L3` doing the same Chroma query as normal search. Public’s resume path uses anchor-scoped `memory_search`, auto-resume heuristics, structural freshness, CocoIndex availability, and a shared payload contract.
- **Why it matters for us**: The MemPalace stack is a good human-readable startup shape, but not a deeper lifecycle system than what Public already has. The reusable part is the compact “identity + essential story + on-demand deepening” packaging, not the underlying retrieval/state model.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 3: MemPalace’s strongest lifecycle contribution is forced save timing before loss, not better saved-state structure
- **Source**: [hooks/README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/hooks/README.md#L7), [mempal_save_hook.sh](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/hooks/mempal_save_hook.sh#L55), [mempal_precompact_hook.sh](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/hooks/mempal_precompact_hook.sh#L49), [generate-context.js](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js#L85), [spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js#L396) `source-confirmed`
- **What it does**: MemPalace’s Stop hook blocks every `SAVE_INTERVAL` human turns and its PreCompact hook always blocks, emitting a system reason that tells the agent to save before stopping or compaction. Public has a strong explicit save path in `generate-context.js` and compaction-safe transport injection, but no equivalent forced preservation checkpoint before context loss.
- **Why it matters for us**: This is the highest-leverage lifecycle idea in the repo. Public already survives compaction structurally, but MemPalace addresses a different failure mode: the user or agent simply forgetting to persist key session context before it disappears.
- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 4: MemPalace session state is intentionally light; Public already has the stronger crash-recovery and trusted-session model
- **Source**: [mempal_save_hook.sh](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/hooks/mempal_save_hook.sh#L85), [mempal_precompact_hook.sh](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/hooks/mempal_precompact_hook.sh#L57), [memory-context.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-context.ts#L821), [context-server.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L1208), [context-server.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L1789) `source-confirmed`
- **What it does**: MemPalace tracks save cycles with `stop_hook_active`, transcript counts, session IDs, and per-session last-save files under `~/.mempalace/hook_state/`. Public resolves trusted session IDs, preserves session lifecycle metadata, recovers interrupted sessions, recovers pending memory files on startup, and resets incomplete ingest jobs after crashes.
- **Why it matters for us**: Public should not simplify its lifecycle state down to MemPalace’s hook-counter model. If we copy the hook timing idea, it should sit on top of the current trusted session/recovery stack rather than replace it.

- **Impact**: high

### Finding 5: MemPalace leaves save-time organization to the agent or heuristics; Public’s structured save contract is more auditable and safer
- **Source**: [miner.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/miner.py#L278), [miner.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/miner.py#L325), [convo_miner.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/convo_miner.py#L290), [general_extractor.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/general_extractor.py#L363), [onboarding.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/onboarding.py#L266), [generate-context.js](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js#L85) `source-confirmed`

Public’s `generate-context.js` is explicitly JSON-primary, treats caller-provided structured session data as authoritative, and preserves exact target-folder authority plus tool/exchange/preflight/postflight context.
- **Why it matters for us**: For lifecycle correctness, Public’s save path is currently better. MemPalace’s free-form save timing is useful, but its save semantics are less auditable than our structured context handoff.
