     1	---
     2	title: "CocoIndex Code - Semantic Code Search"
     3	description: "Semantic code search via vector embeddings. CocoIndex Code enables natural-language discovery of code, patterns, and implementations across 28+ languages using a CLI and a single MCP search tool."
     4	trigger_phrases:
     5	  - "semantic search"
     6	  - "cocoindex"
     7	  - "ccc"
     8	  - "find code that"
     9	  - "code search"
    10	  - "vector search"
    11	  - "find similar code"
    12	---
    13	
    14	# CocoIndex Code - Semantic Code Search
    15	
    16	> Find code by what it does, not what it says: natural-language queries resolved to semantically relevant results across 28+ languages via CLI or a single MCP tool.
    17	
    18	---
    19	
    20	<!-- ANCHOR:table-of-contents -->
    21	## TABLE OF CONTENTS
    22	
    23	- [1. OVERVIEW](#1-overview)
    24	- [2. QUICK START](#2-quick-start)
    25	- [3. FEATURES](#3-features)
    26	- [4. STRUCTURE](#4-structure)
    27	- [5. CONFIGURATION](#5-configuration)
    28	- [6. USAGE EXAMPLES](#6-usage-examples)
    29	- [7. TROUBLESHOOTING](#7-troubleshooting)
    30	- [8. FAQ](#8-faq)
    31	- [9. RELATED DOCUMENTS](#9-related-documents)
    32	
    33	<!-- /ANCHOR:table-of-contents -->
    34	
    35	---
    36	
    37	<!-- ANCHOR:overview -->
    38	## 1. OVERVIEW
    39	
    40	### What This Skill Does
    41	
    42	CocoIndex Code is a semantic code search tool built on vector embeddings. Where `grep` matches exact characters, CocoIndex Code matches meaning. Ask for "retry logic with exponential backoff" and it returns code that implements that pattern, regardless of how the author named variables or functions. This makes it the right tool when you know what a piece of code does but not where it lives or what it is called.
    43	
    44	The skill ships with two access modes. The CLI (`ccc`) is fastest for one-off queries and all index management operations. The MCP server (`ccc mcp`) exposes a single `search` tool that AI agents call directly via stdio transport, integrating semantic search into any tool-calling workflow without leaving the conversation.
    45	
    46	Indexing is incremental and daemon-backed. The first run scans and embeds all supported files in the project. Subsequent runs update only changed files. A background daemon starts automatically on the first command, persists across calls, and restarts itself when settings or the binary version change.
    47	
    48	### Key Statistics
    49	
    50	| Property | Value |
    51	|---|---|
    52	| Version | 1.0.0 |
    53	| MCP tools exposed | 1 (`search`) |
    54	| Supported languages | 28+ |
    55	| Default embedding model | `sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2` (local, no API key) |
    56	| Primary embedding model | `voyage/voyage-code-3` via LiteLLM (1024-dim, requires `VOYAGE_API_KEY`) |
    57	| Vector storage | SQLite via sqlite-vec |
    58	| Chunk size | 1000 chars, 250 char minimum, 150 char overlap |
    59	| Similarity metric | Cosine similarity (0.0 to 1.0) |
    60	
    61	### How This Compares
    62	
    63	| Tool | Use When | Limitation |
    64	|---|---|---|
    65	| `ccc search` (CocoIndex) | You know what code does but not where it lives | Approximate, needs verification |
    66	| `code_graph_query` | You need exact callers, imports, or structural dependencies | Requires the structural graph to be indexed first |
    67	| `Grep` | You know the exact text, symbol, or regex pattern | Cannot find conceptual matches |
    68	| `Glob` | You know the file name or extension pattern | Cannot search file contents |
    69	| `Read` | You know the exact file path | No search capability |
    70	
    71	### Key Features
    72	
    73	| Feature | Description |
    74	|---|---|
    75	| Semantic search | Query by concept or intent, not exact text |
    76	| CLI and MCP modes | `ccc` for terminal use, `ccc mcp` for AI agent integration |
    77	| Language filters | `--lang` (CLI) or `languages` (MCP) narrows results by language |
    78	| Path filters | `--path` (CLI) or `paths` (MCP) scopes results to a directory |
    79	| Incremental indexing | Only re-embeds changed files on subsequent runs |
    80	| Daemon architecture | Auto-starts, auto-restarts on version or settings change |
    81	| Spec Kit integration | Companion lifecycle tools (`ccc_status`, `ccc_reindex`, `ccc_feedback`) and code-graph/session integration are available through system-spec-kit |
    82	| Two embedding models | Local (no API key) or cloud (higher quality) |
    83	| 28+ languages | Language-aware chunk splitting preserves function and class boundaries |
    84	
    85	In the broader system-spec-kit stack, CocoIndex is the semantic half of a three-system retrieval model: CocoIndex finds conceptually similar code, Code Graph answers structural questions, and session bootstrap surfaces CocoIndex readiness during recovery. The companion lifecycle helpers exposed through system-spec-kit are `ccc_status`, `ccc_reindex`, and `ccc_feedback`.
    86	
    87	<!-- /ANCHOR:overview -->
    88	
    89	---
    90	
    91	<!-- ANCHOR:quick-start -->
    92	## 2. QUICK START
    93	
    94	**Step 1: Install and verify**
    95	
    96	Run the idempotent bootstrap helper. It installs the binary, initializes the project if needed, and reports readiness.
    97	
    98	```bash
    99	bash .opencode/skill/mcp-coco-index/scripts/ensure_ready.sh --strict --require-config
   100	```
   101	
   102	**Step 2: Build the index**
   103	
   104	Run from the project root. The first run scans all supported files and generates embeddings. It takes 1-5 minutes depending on codebase size.
   105	
   106	```bash
   107	ccc index
   108	```
   109	
   110	**Step 3: Check index status**
   111	
   112	Confirm files are indexed before searching.
   113	
   114	```bash
   115	ccc status
   116	```
   117	
   118	**Step 4: Search**
   119	
   120	Run a natural-language query. Add language and path filters to narrow results.

 succeeded in 0ms:
 15678	### Finding 1: Engram is built as a store-centered hub with thin delivery surfaces
 15679	- **Source**: [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/cmd/engram/main.go#L113](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/cmd/engram/main.go#L113); [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md#L119](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md#L119)
 15680	- **What it does**: `cmd/engram/main.go` dispatches every surface from one binary: `serve`, `mcp`, `tui`, `search`, `save`, `context`, `sync`, `setup`. The architecture doc matches that by making `internal/store/store.go` the core, with MCP, HTTP, sync, setup, and TUI as adapters around it.
 15681	- **Why it matters for us**: Public already has richer retrieval than Engram, but its memory system spans many MCP tools, scripts, hooks, and supporting subsystems. Engram’s clearer “one core store, many thin surfaces” boundary is a useful architecture lens for reducing wrapper drift.
 15682	- **Recommendation**: prototype later
 15683	- **Impact**: medium
 15684	
 15685	### Finding 2: Engram explicitly separates agent-safe MCP tools from admin/curation tools
 15686	- **Source**: [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/mcp/mcp.go#L50](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/mcp/mcp.go#L50); [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/mcp/mcp.go#L124](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/mcp/mcp.go#L124); [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts#L878](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts#L878); [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L851](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L851)
 15687	- **What it does**: Engram defines `ProfileAgent` and `ProfileAdmin`, resolves them through `ResolveTools()`, and pairs that with “core” versus “deferred” tool instructions. Public currently exposes a flat `TOOL_DEFINITIONS` list from the server without an equivalent profile gate.
 15688	- **Why it matters for us**: This is a concrete answer to the “tool clutter” problem. Public has a much broader MCP surface, so profile-aware listing or wrapper-level profiles could reduce prompt noise and lower misuse risk without removing power-user tools.
 15689	- **Recommendation**: adopt now
 15690	- **Impact**: high
 15691	
 15692	### Finding 3: Engram treats sessions, prompts, and summaries as first-class persisted objects
 15693	- **Source**: [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L441](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L441); [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L754](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L754); [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L1613](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L1613); [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/mcp/mcp.go#L460](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/mcp/mcp.go#L460); [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-resume.ts#L400](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-resume.ts#L400); [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts#L163](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts#L163)
 15694	- **What it does**: Engram persists `sessions`, `observations`, and `user_prompts` as separate tables, exposes `mem_session_start`, `mem_session_end`, and `mem_session_summary`, then composes startup context by formatting recent sessions, prompts, and observations together. Public has strong resume/bootstrap orchestration, but it is centered on recovery composition rather than a lightweight explicit session-start/session-end API.
 15695	- **Why it matters for us**: Public is already stronger at startup recovery and structural readiness, but Engram’s explicit lifecycle tools would add a simpler durable narrative boundary around a session. That is especially useful for compaction survival and for cleaner prompt/session/history analytics.
 15696	- **Recommendation**: adopt now
 15697	- **Impact**: high
 15698	
 15699	### Finding 4: Engram’s memory hygiene revolves around stable topic threads plus duplicate suppression
 15700	- **Source**: [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L948](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L948); [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L3198](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L3198); [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md#L86](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md#L86); [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/memory/generate-context.ts#L75](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/memory/generate-context.ts#L75); [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-save.ts#L1078](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-save.ts#L1078)
 15701	- **What it does**: `AddObservation()` first upserts on `topic_key + project + scope`, incrementing `revision_count`; if no topic match exists, it checks a rolling duplicate window and increments `duplicate_count` instead of inserting a new row. Public’s save path is file-first and quality-gated through `generate-context.ts` and `memory_save`, but it does not expose a comparable first-class thread-key convention for evolving topics.
 15702	- **Why it matters for us**: This is one of Engram’s strongest durable ideas. Public already preserves richer markdown memory artifacts, but long-running decisions and repeated refinements would benefit from an optional stable thread key so related saves revise one conceptual thread instead of fragmenting across many files.
 15703	- **Recommendation**: prototype later
 15704	- **Impact**: high
 15705	
 15706	### Finding 5: Engram’s search is intentionally simple: direct topic-key shortcut first, sanitized FTS5 second
 15707	- **Source**: [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L1462](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L1462); [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L3382](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L3382); [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L482](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L482); [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-context.ts#L1078](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-context.ts#L1078)
 15708	- **What it does**: If the query contains `/`, Engram tries direct `topic_key` lookup first and assigns those hits a dominant rank, then falls back to quoted FTS5 `MATCH` ordered by `fts.rank`. Public’s `memory_search` is much broader: vector + lexical + rerank + intent routing + session/causal boosts, while `memory_context` adds orchestration and graph-aware routing.
 15709	- **Why it matters for us**: Engram’s lexical path is not better than Public’s hybrid stack overall, but its exact-thread shortcut is useful. The idea to copy is the deterministic “if this looks like a thread key, bypass normal ranking,” not the FTS-only architecture.
 15710	- **Recommendation**: prototype later
 15711	- **Impact**: medium
 15712	
 15713	### Finding 6: Agent-agnostic adoption is driven by setup/install code and hook-level protocol injection, not by core-store complexity
 15714	- **Source**: [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/setup/setup.go#L1](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/setup/setup.go#L1); [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/plugin/opencode/engram.ts#L1](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/plugin/opencode/engram.ts#L1); [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/plugin/claude-code/scripts/session-start.sh#L1](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/plugin/claude-code/scripts/session-start.sh#L1); [/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/README.md#L45](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/README.md#L45)
 15715	- **What it does**: `setup.go` writes/install-configures agent-specific MCP integrations for OpenCode, Claude Code, Gemini CLI, and Codex. The OpenCode adapter is explicitly “thin,” and the Claude session-start hook starts the server, creates a session, imports sync state, fetches context, and injects protocol text on startup.
 15716	- **Why it matters for us**: The strong pattern here is distribution discipline: keep setup friction low, keep hooks small, and keep behavioral protocol near the runtime seam. Public already supports more advanced runtime recovery, but Engram’s packaging is cleaner and easier to reason about.
 15717	- **Recommendation**: prototype later
 15718	- **Impact**: medium

  9222	### Finding 1: Mex’s real innovation is a separate knowledge control plane, not markdown storage alone
  9223	- **Source**: [external/README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/README.md#L32), [external/ROUTER.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/ROUTER.md#L56), [external/src/cli.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/cli.ts#L28), [external/src/drift/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/index.ts#L17), [external/src/sync/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/index.ts#L29)
  9224	- **What it does**: Mex explicitly splits the system into `init` pre-analysis, `check` static drift detection, `sync` targeted repair, and `pattern add` scaffold growth. `ROUTER.md` then turns that into an operating loop: `CONTEXT -> BUILD -> VERIFY -> DEBUG -> GROW`. The important part is that markdown is not trusted by default; it is continuously audited and selectively repaired.
  9225	- **Why it matters for us**: Spec Kit Memory is strong at retrieval, resume, trigger recall, and save-time quality, but it does not yet have a dedicated repo-wide “is our durable project truth still true?” plane. Mex adds that missing authority surface.
  9226	- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
  9227	- **Impact**: high
  9228	
  9229	### Finding 2: The hinge is claim extraction: Mex converts prose into typed, checkable assertions
  9230	- **Source**: [external/src/drift/claims.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/claims.ts#L36), [external/src/drift/checkers/edges.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/edges.ts#L5), [external/src/drift/checkers/command.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/command.ts#L5), [external/src/drift/checkers/dependency.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/dependency.ts#L38), [external/src/drift/checkers/cross-file.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/cross-file.ts#L3), [external/src/drift/checkers/index-sync.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/index-sync.ts#L6)
  9231	- **What it does**: `extractClaims()` mines inline code, code blocks, bold dependency names, and version phrases into typed claims: `path`, `command`, `dependency`, and `version`. It deliberately skips template placeholders, HTTP routes, code snippets, and negated sections. The checker suite then validates each claim family against disk, frontmatter edges, manifests, and other scaffold files.
  9232	- **Why it matters for us**: Our current guardrails validate memory file quality after write and recover session context well, but they do not parse repo guidance and durable docs into machine-auditable claims. That is the core mechanism we would need if we want automated doc-to-code truth checks rather than just structure checks.
  9233	- **Recommendation**: prototype later
  9234	- **Impact**: high
  9235	
  9236	### Finding 3: Targeted sync is the strongest adoptable pattern because it repairs only the broken knowledge surface
  9237	- **Source**: [external/src/sync/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/index.ts#L47), [external/src/sync/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/index.ts#L61), [external/src/sync/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/index.ts#L144), [external/src/sync/brief-builder.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/brief-builder.ts#L41), [external/src/sync/brief-builder.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/brief-builder.ts#L58), [external/src/sync/brief-builder.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/brief-builder.ts#L99), [external/src/git.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/git.ts#L52), [external/SYNC.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/SYNC.md#L44)
  9238	- **What it does**: `runSync()` reruns drift detection, filters to error-bearing files by default, groups issues per file, and builds a combined brief. That brief includes the exact issue list, current file content, nearby filesystem context for missing paths, and a recent git diff for referenced paths. The repair instruction is explicitly bounded: change only what is necessary, then re-run drift and loop again.
  9239	- **Why it matters for us**: This is the clearest gap in Spec Kit Memory. We have `memory_health`, `session_resume`, `session_bootstrap`, `post-save-review`, and `validate-memory-quality`, but none of them emits a minimal, evidence-rich remediation packet for stale repo guidance, spec docs, or memory-facing documentation. Mex’s repair loop is the most directly portable idea.
  9240	- **Recommendation**: adopt now
  9241	- **Impact**: high
  9242	
  9243	### Finding 4: Mex’s freshness model is useful because it measures both elapsed time and repo churn
  9244	- **Source**: [external/src/drift/checkers/staleness.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/staleness.ts#L4), [external/src/drift/checkers/staleness.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/staleness.ts#L17), [external/src/git.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/git.ts#L12), [external/src/git.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/git.ts#L31), [session-resume.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-resume.ts#L400), [session-bootstrap.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts#L163)
  9245	- **What it does**: Every scaffold file gets two staleness checks: days since last change and commits since last change, with thresholds of `30/90` days and `50/200` commits. That means a doc can be stale because the repo changed around it rapidly even if not much wall-clock time passed.
  9246	- **Why it matters for us**: Our freshness signals today are mostly runtime recovery signals: session continuity, code-graph readiness, CocoIndex availability, database health. Mex suggests a new axis: “durable project truth freshness relative to repo activity.” That complements, rather than replaces, `session_resume` and `session_bootstrap`.
  9247	- **Recommendation**: prototype later
  9248	- **Impact**: medium
  9249	
  9250	### Finding 5: Mex also checks omissions, not just false statements, via reality-to-scaffold coverage
  9251	- **Source**: [external/src/drift/checkers/script-coverage.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/script-coverage.ts#L26), [external/README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/README.md#L72), [memory-crud-health.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts#L222), [post-save-review.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/core/post-save-review.ts#L8), [validate-memory-quality.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/validate-memory-quality.ts#L45)
  9252	- **What it does**: `checkScriptCoverage()` loads `package.json` scripts, ignores lifecycle noise, and warns when a real script is not mentioned anywhere in scaffold text. That is a reverse audit: reality proves docs incomplete, even when docs never made a wrong claim.
  9253	- **Why it matters for us**: Spec Kit’s current checks are stronger on write-time artifact integrity and runtime system health than on omission detection in durable repo guidance. A lightweight “undocumented operational surface” checker would add value, especially for scripts, commands, and perhaps major MCP surfaces. The caveat is that Mex’s implementation is intentionally shallow string matching, so we should copy the pattern, not the exact heuristic.
  9254	- **Recommendation**: prototype later
  9255	- **Impact**: medium
  9256

  9265	
  9266	## Assessment
  9267	- New information ratio: 0.74
  9268	- Questions addressed: strongest end-to-end pattern; how Mex turns markdown into auditable claims; whether targeted sync is meaningfully different from our current health/save surfaces; whether repo-activity freshness is a distinct signal; whether omission detection adds value beyond structural validation.
  9269	- Questions answered: the strongest pattern is a separate doc-truth control plane; targeted sync is the highest-value missing capability for us; claim extraction is the technical hinge that makes the plane possible; dual-signal freshness is worth adding as a new axis; omission checks are useful, but Mex’s exact heuristics should be treated as prototypes, not copied wholesale.
  9270	
  9271	## Reflection
  9272	- What worked: tracing the loop in execution order, `ROUTER.md` contract -> `cli.ts` surfaces -> `claims.ts` extraction -> checker family -> `sync/brief-builder.ts` repair packet -> recheck, made the actual innovation much clearer than reading checker summaries in isolation.
  9273	- What did not work: the prompt’s `external/mex-main` path is stale in this checkout, the real snapshot is `external/`; the strict validator passed, but it still emitted `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`, so the pass signal was clean but noisy.
  9274	
  9275	## Recommended Next Focus
  9276	Compare Modus Memory and Mnemosyne against this same control-plane question: do either of them provide a durable truth-audit surface with bounded remediation, or are they still primarily storage and retrieval systems? The sharpest next cut is provenance and trust: which system, if any, has a stronger answer than Mex for evidence-backed reusable guidance without placeholder-first drift.
  9277

  9283	# Iteration 014: DEEP DIVE - STRONGEST PATTERN
  9284	
  9285	## Focus
  9286	DEEP DIVE - STRONGEST PATTERN: Go deep on the single most impactful pattern from this system. Trace it end-to-end.
  9287	
  9288	## Findings
  9289	### Finding 1: Mex’s real innovation is a separate knowledge control plane, not markdown storage alone
  9290	- **Source**: [external/README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/README.md#L32), [external/ROUTER.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/ROUTER.md#L56), [external/src/cli.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/cli.ts#L28), [external/src/drift/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/index.ts#L17), [external/src/sync/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/index.ts#L29)
  9291	- **What it does**: Mex explicitly splits the system into `init` pre-analysis, `check` static drift detection, `sync` targeted repair, and `pattern add` scaffold growth. `ROUTER.md` then turns that into an operating loop: `CONTEXT -> BUILD -> VERIFY -> DEBUG -> GROW`. The important part is that markdown is not trusted by default; it is continuously audited and selectively repaired.
  9292	- **Why it matters for us**: Spec Kit Memory is strong at retrieval, resume, trigger recall, and save-time quality, but it does not yet have a dedicated repo-wide “is our durable project truth still true?” plane. Mex adds that missing authority surface.
  9293	- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
  9294	- **Impact**: high
  9295	
  9296	### Finding 2: The hinge is claim extraction: Mex converts prose into typed, checkable assertions
  9297	- **Source**: [external/src/drift/claims.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/claims.ts#L36), [external/src/drift/checkers/edges.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/edges.ts#L5), [external/src/drift/checkers/command.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/command.ts#L5), [external/src/drift/checkers/dependency.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/dependency.ts#L38), [external/src/drift/checkers/cross-file.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/cross-file.ts#L3), [external/src/drift/checkers/index-sync.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/index-sync.ts#L6)
  9298	- **What it does**: `extractClaims()` mines inline code, code blocks, bold dependency names, and version phrases into typed claims: `path`, `command`, `dependency`, and `version`. It deliberately skips template placeholders, HTTP routes, code snippets, and negated sections. The checker suite then validates each claim family against disk, frontmatter edges, manifests, and other scaffold files.
  9299	- **Why it matters for us**: Our current guardrails validate memory file quality after write and recover session context well, but they do not parse repo guidance and durable docs into machine-auditable claims. That is the core mechanism we would need if we want automated doc-to-code truth checks rather than just structure checks.
  9300	- **Recommendation**: prototype later
  9301	- **Impact**: high
  9302	
  9303	### Finding 3: Targeted sync is the strongest adoptable pattern because it repairs only the broken knowledge surface
  9304	- **Source**: [external/src/sync/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/index.ts#L47), [external/src/sync/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/index.ts#L61), [external/src/sync/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/index.ts#L144), [external/src/sync/brief-builder.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/brief-builder.ts#L41), [external/src/sync/brief-builder.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/brief-builder.ts#L58), [external/src/sync/brief-builder.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/brief-builder.ts#L99), [external/src/git.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/git.ts#L52), [external/SYNC.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/SYNC.md#L44)
  9305	- **What it does**: `runSync()` reruns drift detection, filters to error-bearing files by default, groups issues per file, and builds a combined brief. That brief includes the exact issue list, current file content, nearby filesystem context for missing paths, and a recent git diff for referenced paths. The repair instruction is explicitly bounded: change only what is necessary, then re-run drift and loop again.
  9306	- **Why it matters for us**: This is the clearest gap in Spec Kit Memory. We have `memory_health`, `session_resume`, `session_bootstrap`, `post-save-review`, and `validate-memory-quality`, but none of them emits a minimal, evidence-rich remediation packet for stale repo guidance, spec docs, or memory-facing documentation. Mex’s repair loop is the most directly portable idea.
  9307	- **Recommendation**: adopt now
  9308	- **Impact**: high
  9309	
  9310	### Finding 4: Mex’s freshness model is useful because it measures both elapsed time and repo churn
  9311	- **Source**: [external/src/drift/checkers/staleness.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/staleness.ts#L4), [external/src/drift/checkers/staleness.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/staleness.ts#L17), [external/src/git.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/git.ts#L12), [external/src/git.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/git.ts#L31), [session-resume.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-resume.ts#L400), [session-bootstrap.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts#L163)
  9312	- **What it does**: Every scaffold file gets two staleness checks: days since last change and commits since last change, with thresholds of `30/90` days and `50/200` commits. That means a doc can be stale because the repo changed around it rapidly even if not much wall-clock time passed.
  9313	- **Why it matters for us**: Our freshness signals today are mostly runtime recovery signals: session continuity, code-graph readiness, CocoIndex availability, database health. Mex suggests a new axis: “durable project truth freshness relative to repo activity.” That complements, rather than replaces, `session_resume` and `session_bootstrap`.
  9314	- **Recommendation**: prototype later
  9315	- **Impact**: medium
  9316	
  9317	### Finding 5: Mex also checks omissions, not just false statements, via reality-to-scaffold coverage
  9318	- **Source**: [external/src/drift/checkers/script-coverage.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/script-coverage.ts#L26), [external/README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/README.md#L72), [memory-crud-health.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts#L222), [post-save-review.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/core/post-save-review.ts#L8), [validate-memory-quality.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/validate-memory-quality.ts#L45)
  9319	- **What it does**: `checkScriptCoverage()` loads `package.json` scripts, ignores lifecycle noise, and warns when a real script is not mentioned anywhere in scaffold text. That is a reverse audit: reality proves docs incomplete, even when docs never made a wrong claim.
  9320	- **Why it matters for us**: Spec Kit’s current checks are stronger on write-time artifact integrity and runtime system health than on omission detection in durable repo guidance. A lightweight “undocumented operational surface” checker would add value, especially for scripts, commands, and perhaps major MCP surfaces. The caveat is that Mex’s implementation is intentionally shallow string matching, so we should copy the pattern, not the exact heuristic.

  5408	
  5409	## Findings
  5410	
  5411	### Finding 1: Hook-enforced pre-compaction preservation is the strongest transferable pattern
  5412	- **Source**: [mempal_save_hook.sh#L85](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/hooks/mempal_save_hook.sh#L85), [mempal_precompact_hook.sh#L57](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/hooks/mempal_precompact_hook.sh#L57), [spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js#L396](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js#L396), [generate-context.js#L85](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js#L85)
  5413	- **What it does**: MemPalace blocks on two timing edges: after enough user turns it emits a save instruction, and before compaction it always blocks and can optionally run synchronous ingest before context shrinks. Public already has a compaction hook surface, but it currently injects structural transport context rather than triggering a governed memory save.
  5414	- **Why it matters for us**: This is the cleanest integration candidate because Public already has the compaction interception point and already prefers JSON-primary saves. Integration difficulty is **medium**: the hook surface exists, but we would need a strict contract so compaction can only trigger structured `generate-context.js` style evidence capture, not MemPalace’s free-form “save everything” prompt.
  5415	- **Recommendation**: prototype later
  5416	- **Impact**: high
  5417	
  5418	### Finding 2: Status-as-protocol bootstrap is the easiest thing to port safely
  5419	- **Source**: [mcp_server.py#L139](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/mcp_server.py#L139), [mcp_server.py#L169](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/mcp_server.py#L169), [context-server.ts#L759](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L759), [context-server.ts#L782](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L782)
  5420	- **What it does**: `mempalace_status` does more than health reporting: it returns the palace overview plus `PALACE_PROTOCOL` and `AAAK_SPEC`, explicitly teaching the agent when to query, when to write diaries, and when to invalidate facts. Public already emits recovery and routing guidance in `context-server.ts`, but not a short memory-discipline protocol this direct.
  5421	- **Why it matters for us**: Public already has the right bootstrap surfaces, so this is a **low** integration lift. A small “memory operating protocol” added to `session_bootstrap` or an equivalent status surface would reinforce verify-before-answer and recovery order without importing MemPalace’s heavier framing.
  5422	- **Recommendation**: adopt now
  5423	- **Impact**: medium
  5424	
  5425	### Finding 3: The L0/L1/L2/L3 stack is reusable only as a thin digest, not as a full model
  5426	- **Source**: [layers.py#L34](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/layers.py#L34), [layers.py#L76](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/layers.py#L76), [layers.py#L196](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/layers.py#L196), [layers.py#L389](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/layers.py#L389), [README.md#L275](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/README.md#L275), [tool-schemas.ts#L740](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts#L740)
  5427	- **What it does**: In code, L0 is a static `~/.mempalace/identity.txt`, L1 is a capped summary generated from the top 15 drawers by simple importance sorting, L2 is filtered `get`, and L3 is the same Chroma semantic search. The implementation says wake-up is roughly 600-900 tokens, not the much lighter story implied in the README table.
  5428	- **Why it matters for us**: Public already has `session_bootstrap`, `session_resume`, `memory_context(resume)`, and `memory_match_triggers`, so a full port would duplicate stronger existing recovery paths with a weaker ranking heuristic. Integration difficulty is **medium-high**. The reusable part is only a thin, project-scoped startup digest layer, not the entire MemPalace layering model.
  5429	- **Recommendation**: prototype later
  5430	- **Impact**: medium
  5431	
  5432	### Finding 4: Full palace taxonomy is expensive to integrate and not differentiated enough
  5433	- **Source**: [miner.py#L278](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/miner.py#L278), [room_detector_local.py#L120](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/room_detector_local.py#L120), [searcher.py#L21](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/searcher.py#L21), [palace_graph.py#L34](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/palace_graph.py#L34), [README.md#L52](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/README.md#L52), [BENCHMARKS.md#L602](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/benchmarks/BENCHMARKS.md#L602)
  5434	- **What it does**: MemPalace room assignment is mostly folder-name and keyword heuristics at ingest, plain Chroma metadata filters at retrieval, and a derived graph built from shared `wing`/`room` metadata. The README’s own correction note says the “+34% palace boost” is just metadata filtering, and the benchmark notes show room routing still trails the no-rooms hybrid baseline.
  5435	- **Why it matters for us**: Public would need new save-time taxonomy generation, label governance, reindex logic, and drift controls just to recreate a layer that is mostly packaging plus filters. Integration difficulty is **high**, and the unique upside is too small given Public already has spec-folder scoping, context types, tiers, and structural code graph tools.
  5436	- **Recommendation**: reject
  5437	- **Impact**: low
  5438	
  5439	### Finding 5: A separate temporal fact graph is valuable, but only behind a stricter source contract than MemPalace uses
  5440	- **Source**: [knowledge_graph.py#L46](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/knowledge_graph.py#L46), [knowledge_graph.py#L121](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/knowledge_graph.py#L121), [knowledge_graph.py#L180](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/knowledge_graph.py#L180), [knowledge_graph.py#L196](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/knowledge_graph.py#L196), [knowledge_graph.py#L282](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/knowledge_graph.py#L282), [memory-search.ts#L482](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L482)
  5441	- **What it does**: MemPalace keeps a separate SQLite fact store with `entities`, `triples`, `valid_from`, and `valid_to`, plus dedicated add/query/invalidate/timeline operations. That separation is real and useful: it prevents temporal facts from being conflated with raw drawer retrieval.
  5442	- **Why it matters for us**: Public currently has strong recall/search and causal links, but not a fact plane with temporal invalidation semantics. Integration difficulty is **high** because this would need governed fact extraction, citation requirements, correction workflows, and clear authority boundaries between facts and verbatim memories. The architecture is worth exploring, but only as a stricter Public-native subsystem.
  5443	- **Recommendation**: prototype later
  5444	- **Impact**: high
  5445

# Iteration 013: GAP ANALYSIS - PARADIGM SHIFTS

## Focus
GAP ANALYSIS - PARADIGM SHIFTS: What paradigm shifts does this system suggest for our memory architecture?

## Findings

### Finding 1: **Modus treats memory as a multi-plane operating system, not one indexed corpus**
- **Source**: `external/internal/learnings/learnings.go:1-10,23-64,121-208,299-328`; `external/internal/vault/prs.go:10-46`; `external/internal/vault/missions.go:14-39,89-111,211-256`; `external/internal/index/crossref.go:9-16,154-214,221-245`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:41-45,48-188,275-399,471-499`
- **What it does**: Modus splits memory into distinct planes with different lifecycles: `brain/learnings` for cross-model operational lessons, `atlas/prs` for proposal artifacts, `missions/*` for execution state, and `atlas/*` for entity/belief knowledge. `crossref.go` then federates those planes into one neighborhood query without flattening them into one storage contract. Public’s exposed surface is broader in raw tooling, but it is still centered on one generic memory substrate plus adjunct mutation, checkpoint, causal, and pre/postflight tools.
- **Why it matters for us**: This is the clearest paradigm shift in the codebase. The next architecture step for Public is likely **not another retrieval feature**, but separating “retrieved knowledge,” “operational lessons,” “governed assertions,” and “execution state” into first-class memory planes with different mutation and surfacing rules.
- **Recommendation**: **NEW FEATURE**
- **Impact**: **high**

### Finding 2: **Governance should include an explicit autonomy stage, not just access control**
- **Source**: `external/internal/vault/trust.go:11-25,28-95`; `external/internal/mcp/vault.go:607-635`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:275-399,402-466`
- **What it does**: Modus persists a trust stage in `atlas/trust.md`, defaults to stage 1 when absent, forbids self-promotion in code comments and tool descriptions, and appends human-readable transition history on every stage change. The stage changes runtime posture: Inform, Recommend, or Act. Public’s governance surface is strong on tenant/shared-space access and safe mutation boundaries, but that is **scope governance**, not **autonomy governance**.
- **Why it matters for us**: Public currently decides “can this caller touch this memory?” but not “may the system autonomously execute, only recommend, or only observe?” Modus suggests adding an operator-owned autonomy layer above mutation tools, reconsolidation actions, and future approval workflows.
- **Recommendation**: **prototype later**
- **Impact**: **high**

### Finding 3: **High-risk memory changes should pass through a durable proposal inbox**
- **Source**: `external/internal/vault/prs.go:10-110`; `external/internal/mcp/vault.go:681-777`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/save/reconsolidation-bridge.ts:64-131`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:291-322`
- **What it does**: Modus opens persistent PR artifacts with `target_type`, `target_id`, `reasoning`, `confidence`, and `linked_belief_ids`; merge/reject is explicit and feeds back into belief confidence. Public already has the raw ingredients for this pattern: assistive reconsolidation classifies borderline pairs and emits review-tier recommendations, but today those remain console warnings while actual changes still happen via direct `memory_update` / `memory_delete` / bulk-delete flows.
- **Why it matters for us**: This is an immediately actionable shift. Public does not need Modus’s whole atlas model to benefit; it can turn its existing shadow recommendations into durable proposal rows with accept/reject outcomes, creating a real operator inbox for reconsolidation, supersession, and possibly promotion/deprecation decisions.
- **Recommendation**: **adopt now**
- **Impact**: **high**

### Finding 4: **Decay should drive visible workflow states, not stay buried inside scoring and cleanup**
- **Source**: `external/internal/vault/facts.go:160-254`; `external/internal/vault/beliefs.go:31-126`; `external/internal/learnings/learnings.go:229-277`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts:519-520`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/stage2-fusion.ts:840-883,1194-1207`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/feedback/batch-learning.ts:4-20,33-41,175-247`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/checkpoints.ts:721-777`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/archival-manager.ts:357-445,670-684`
- **What it does**: In Modus, recall and review outcomes change named artifact state directly: facts reinforce and later archive, beliefs decay/reinforce/weaken, learnings deprecate by zeroing confidence, and PR outcomes feed back into linked beliefs. Public has equivalent mechanics scattered across hidden layers: `trackAccess` is opt-in and off by default, FSRS write-back lives inside Stage 2, batch learning is explicitly shadow-only, negative validation adjusts scoring confidence, and archival runs as a background subsystem.
- **Why it matters for us**: The shift is from “decay as ranking math” to “decay as memory operations workflow.” Public already has most primitives, but not a first-class stale/review/promote/deprecate surface that operators can inspect and triage. That missing workflow layer is now a bigger gap than the decay math itself.
- **Recommendation**: **NEW FEATURE**
- **Impact**: **high**

### Finding 5: **Memory can become an execution-readiness layer, not just a recall layer**
- **Source**: `external/internal/vault/missions.go:153-256`; `external/internal/mcp/vault.go:781-845`; `external/internal/index/crossref.go:154-214,263-278`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:471-499`
- **What it does**: Modus stores missions as markdown objects with typed dependencies (`blocks`, `informs`, `enhances`), cycle checks, satisfaction status, and `CanStart` readiness logic surfaced through MCP. Connected search can then return missions alongside facts, beliefs, and learnings. Public’s nearest analogue is `task_preflight` / `task_postflight`, but those record epistemic telemetry after or before work; they do not create a live ready/blocked execution graph.
- **Why it matters for us**: This suggests a second major shift: memory should not only help answer questions; it should also expose what work is blocked, what is ready, and what knowledge dependencies remain unresolved. That would pair naturally with the proposal inbox and review queue ideas.
- **Recommendation**: **prototype later**
- **Impact**: **medium**

## Sources Consulted
- `external/internal/learnings/learnings.go`
- `external/internal/mcp/learnings.go`
- `external/internal/mcp/vault.go`
- `external/internal/vault/trust.go`
- `external/internal/vault/prs.go`
- `external/internal/vault/beliefs.go`
- `external/internal/vault/entities.go`
- `external/internal/vault/missions.go`
- `external/internal/vault/facts.go`
- `external/internal/index/crossref.go`
- `external/internal/index/facts.go`
- `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts`
- `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts`
- `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/stage2-fusion.ts`
- `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/save/reconsolidation-bridge.ts`
- `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/feedback/batch-learning.ts`
- `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/checkpoints.ts`
- `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/archival-manager.ts`
- `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/save/create-record.ts`
- `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/fsrs-scheduler.ts`
- `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/fsrs.ts`
- `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/hooks/memory-surface.ts`
- `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts`

## Assessment
- New information ratio: **0.76**
- Questions addressed: whether Modus’s non-search modules imply a different memory architecture; whether Public’s largest remaining gaps are workflow/governance rather than retrieval; whether trust stages, PRs, and missions form a true control plane; whether decay should become an operator-visible state machine.
- Questions answered: **yes** — the strongest paradigm shifts are **multi-plane memory architecture, autonomy-stage governance, proposal-first mutation, decay-as-workflow, and readiness-aware mission state**. The delta is now more about **memory operations architecture** than about BM25 or FSRS formulas.

## Reflection
- What worked: Tracing `internal/mcp/vault.go` back into `trust.go`, `prs.go`, `missions.go`, and `learnings.go` made the architectural boundaries obvious very quickly. The cleanest comparison came from matching those tool surfaces against Public’s authoritative MCP schemas and mutation handlers.
- What did not work: Looking at beliefs/entities in isolation was too low-level; the paradigm shift only became visible once the operator-facing surfaces were included. Search-only comparisons would have understated the real gap.

## Recommended Next Focus
Investigate a system with an explicit **review queue / due-items scheduler / approval inbox** so the next pass can test whether Public now needs a dedicated triage layer above proposals, decay signals, and mission readiness rather than more retrieval improvements.

18:You are a research specialist in hybrid search architectures (BM25 + vector embeddings), OpenCode plugin design, memory scoping patterns, and session compaction resilience. Work like a systems analyst who can separate TypeScript wrapper behavior from backend README claims, trace plugin lifecycle and hook wiring precisely, and translate OpenCode Mnemosyne's concrete design into practical improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`.
22:Research OpenCode Mnemosyne's hybrid search architecture, plugin design patterns, and compaction survival strategies to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around combining text and vector search, OpenCode plugin integration, project-vs-global memory scoping, and preserving memory awareness across context compaction. Focus on what the external repo actually proves in `src/index.ts` and what the README describes about the Mnemosyne backend. Classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
28:OpenCode Mnemosyne is an OpenCode plugin that wraps the Go-based Mnemosyne local memory backend. The external repo is intentionally thin: `src/index.ts` contains the plugin entry point, CLI bridge, tool registration, scope initialization, and the `experimental.session.compacting` hook, while `README.md` describes the backend's hybrid retrieval model and intended agent workflow. The documented retrieval stack combines full-text search (SQLite FTS5 with BM25 ranking) and vector search (sqlite-vec cosine similarity using `snowflake-arctic-embed-m-v1.5`), then fuses both with Reciprocal Rank Fusion. All inference is local, the ML model is downloaded on first use (~500 MB), and no cloud APIs are involved.
30:The plugin exposes five memory tools: `memory_recall`, `memory_recall_global`, `memory_store`, `memory_store_global`, and `memory_delete`. Project scope is derived from the current directory name and auto-initialized during plugin load; the global collection is initialized lazily on first global write. The wrapper shells out to the `mnemosyne` binary via `Bun.spawn`, quotes search strings to avoid FTS syntax issues, and injects compact memory-usage guidance into the compaction prompt so agents retain memory awareness after context resets.
43:`Code_Environment/Public` already has Spec Kit Memory with a hybrid vector/BM25 memory retrieval pipeline in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts`, startup and resume flows in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts`, and CocoIndex for semantic code search through `.opencode/skill/mcp-coco-index/README.md`. It also already has a compaction-oriented transport plugin in `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js` that injects structural context during `experimental.session.compacting`.
45:What this repo does **not** currently have is an OpenCode memory plugin that wraps a standalone local binary, a first-class project/global tool pair with Mnemosyne's lightweight ergonomics, or a memory-specific compaction hook that injects tool-usage guidance directly into the compaction prompt. Do not repeat the outdated claim that Public lacks hybrid BM25+vector memory retrieval; compare Mnemosyne against the current code, not stale assumptions.
56:8. Examine the `experimental.session.compacting` hook implementation in `src/index.ts` line by line. Treat this as the most novel feature of the phase: capture exactly what guidance is injected, when it runs, and how that compares with Public's structural compaction plugin in `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js`.
57:9. Only after the wrapper analysis, read `external/opencode-mnemosyne-main/README.md`. Use it to extract hybrid-search claims, model choice, AGENTS.md guidance, offline-first positioning, and the stated project/global collection model. Distinguish clearly between wrapper-verified behavior and backend claims that are only documented here.
59:11. Compare Mnemosyne directly against current `Code_Environment/Public` code: `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts` for existing hybrid retrieval, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts` for session bootstrap/resume behavior, `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js` for compaction injection, and `.opencode/skill/mcp-coco-index/README.md` for semantic code-search positioning.
60:12. Resolve cross-phase boundaries explicitly. Do not redo phase `001` as generic FTS5 study or phase `003` as generic BM25 study. This phase owns wrapper-to-backend boundaries, vector-search implications, OpenCode plugin ergonomics, project/global scoping, AGENTS guidance, and memory-awareness compaction behavior.

2076:The two embedding models trade off quality against convenience. The local model (`all-MiniLM-L6-v2`) requires no API key and works offline, making it the right default for most projects. The Voyage Code 3 model produces 1024-dimensional vectors trained specifically on code, and consistently returns higher-quality results for complex queries on large codebases. Switching models requires a full reset and reindex because the vector dimensions are incompatible.

3748:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:128:### Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF)
3749:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:132:**Why RRF?**
3750:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:135:- Citation: Cormack et al. "RRF outperforms Condorcet" (SIGIR 2009)
3751:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:140:- **Adaptive Fusion**: Intent-aware weighted RRF with dark-run mode (feature flag `SPECKIT_ADAPTIVE_FUSION`)
3753:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:148:// RRF score = 1/(40+2) + 1/(40+5) + 1.5/(40+1)

4947:You are a research specialist in hybrid search architectures (BM25 + vector embeddings), OpenCode plugin design, memory scoping patterns, and session compaction resilience. Work like a systems analyst who can separate TypeScript wrapper behavior from backend README claims, trace plugin lifecycle and hook wiring precisely, and translate OpenCode Mnemosyne's concrete design into practical improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`.
4951:Research OpenCode Mnemosyne's hybrid search architecture, plugin design patterns, and compaction survival strategies to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around combining text and vector search, OpenCode plugin integration, project-vs-global memory scoping, and preserving memory awareness across context compaction. Focus on what the external repo actually proves in `src/index.ts` and what the README describes about the Mnemosyne backend. Classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
4957:OpenCode Mnemosyne is an OpenCode plugin that wraps the Go-based Mnemosyne local memory backend. The external repo is intentionally thin: `src/index.ts` contains the plugin entry point, CLI bridge, tool registration, scope initialization, and the `experimental.session.compacting` hook, while `README.md` describes the backend's hybrid retrieval model and intended agent workflow. The documented retrieval stack combines full-text search (SQLite FTS5 with BM25 ranking) and vector search (sqlite-vec cosine similarity using `snowflake-arctic-embed-m-v1.5`), then fuses both with Reciprocal Rank Fusion. All inference is local, the ML model is downloaded on first use (~500 MB), and no cloud APIs are involved.
4959:The plugin exposes five memory tools: `memory_recall`, `memory_recall_global`, `memory_store`, `memory_store_global`, and `memory_delete`. Project scope is derived from the current directory name and auto-initialized during plugin load; the global collection is initialized lazily on first global write. The wrapper shells out to the `mnemosyne` binary via `Bun.spawn`, quotes search strings to avoid FTS syntax issues, and injects compact memory-usage guidance into the compaction prompt so agents retain memory awareness after context resets.
4972:`Code_Environment/Public` already has Spec Kit Memory with a hybrid vector/BM25 memory retrieval pipeline in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts`, startup and resume flows in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts`, and CocoIndex for semantic code search through `.opencode/skill/mcp-coco-index/README.md`. It also already has a compaction-oriented transport plugin in `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js` that injects structural context during `experimental.session.compacting`.
4974:What this repo does **not** currently have is an OpenCode memory plugin that wraps a standalone local binary, a first-class project/global tool pair with Mnemosyne's lightweight ergonomics, or a memory-specific compaction hook that injects tool-usage guidance directly into the compaction prompt. Do not repeat the outdated claim that Public lacks hybrid BM25+vector memory retrieval; compare Mnemosyne against the current code, not stale assumptions.
4985:8. Examine the `experimental.session.compacting` hook implementation in `src/index.ts` line by line. Treat this as the most novel feature of the phase: capture exactly what guidance is injected, when it runs, and how that compares with Public's structural compaction plugin in `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js`.
4986:9. Only after the wrapper analysis, read `external/opencode-mnemosyne-main/README.md`. Use it to extract hybrid-search claims, model choice, AGENTS.md guidance, offline-first positioning, and the stated project/global collection model. Distinguish clearly between wrapper-verified behavior and backend claims that are only documented here.
4988:11. Compare Mnemosyne directly against current `Code_Environment/Public` code: `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts` for existing hybrid retrieval, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts` for session bootstrap/resume behavior, `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js` for compaction injection, and `.opencode/skill/mcp-coco-index/README.md` for semantic code-search positioning.
4989:12. Resolve cross-phase boundaries explicitly. Do not redo phase `001` as generic FTS5 study or phase `003` as generic BM25 study. This phase owns wrapper-to-backend boundaries, vector-search implications, OpenCode plugin ergonomics, project/global scoping, AGENTS guidance, and memory-awareness compaction behavior.
4996:    Research the external repository at /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/opencode-mnemosyne-main and identify concrete improvements for Code_Environment/Public, especially around hybrid search (BM25 + vector embeddings), OpenCode plugin architecture, memory scoping (project vs global), session compaction resilience, and offline-first memory design.

5014:10. What are the practical implications of the offline-first design, including local model download cost, first-run latency, privacy benefits, and failure handling when the binary or model is missing?

5053:**Finding: Memory-tool awareness survives compaction through prompt injection**
5055:- What it does: Mnemosyne appends a compact memory-usage instruction block during `experimental.session.compacting`, while Public's existing compaction plugin injects structural transport context rather than memory-tool reminders.
5056:- Why it matters: Public may already preserve structural state across compaction, but it does not yet remind the agent of concrete memory tools in the same direct way.
5058:- Affected area: compaction strategy, startup guidance, memory-tool ergonomics
