55961:You are a research specialist in hybrid search architectures (BM25 + vector embeddings), OpenCode plugin design, memory scoping patterns, and session compaction resilience. Work like a systems analyst who can separate TypeScript wrapper behavior from backend README claims, trace plugin lifecycle and hook wiring precisely, and translate OpenCode Mnemosyne's concrete design into practical improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`.
55965:Research OpenCode Mnemosyne's hybrid search architecture, plugin design patterns, and compaction survival strategies to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around combining text and vector search, OpenCode plugin integration, project-vs-global memory scoping, and preserving memory awareness across context compaction. Focus on what the external repo actually proves in `src/index.ts` and what the README describes about the Mnemosyne backend. Classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
55971:OpenCode Mnemosyne is an OpenCode plugin that wraps the Go-based Mnemosyne local memory backend. The external repo is intentionally thin: `src/index.ts` contains the plugin entry point, CLI bridge, tool registration, scope initialization, and the `experimental.session.compacting` hook, while `README.md` describes the backend's hybrid retrieval model and intended agent workflow. The documented retrieval stack combines full-text search (SQLite FTS5 with BM25 ranking) and vector search (sqlite-vec cosine similarity using `snowflake-arctic-embed-m-v1.5`), then fuses both with Reciprocal Rank Fusion. All inference is local, the ML model is downloaded on first use (~500 MB), and no cloud APIs are involved.
55973:The plugin exposes five memory tools: `memory_recall`, `memory_recall_global`, `memory_store`, `memory_store_global`, and `memory_delete`. Project scope is derived from the current directory name and auto-initialized during plugin load; the global collection is initialized lazily on first global write. The wrapper shells out to the `mnemosyne` binary via `Bun.spawn`, quotes search strings to avoid FTS syntax issues, and injects compact memory-usage guidance into the compaction prompt so agents retain memory awareness after context resets.
55986:`Code_Environment/Public` already has Spec Kit Memory with a hybrid vector/BM25 memory retrieval pipeline in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts`, startup and resume flows in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts`, and CocoIndex for semantic code search through `.opencode/skill/mcp-coco-index/README.md`. It also already has a compaction-oriented transport plugin in `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js` that injects structural context during `experimental.session.compacting`.
55988:What this repo does **not** currently have is an OpenCode memory plugin that wraps a standalone local binary, a first-class project/global tool pair with Mnemosyne's lightweight ergonomics, or a memory-specific compaction hook that injects tool-usage guidance directly into the compaction prompt. Do not repeat the outdated claim that Public lacks hybrid BM25+vector memory retrieval; compare Mnemosyne against the current code, not stale assumptions.
55999:8. Examine the `experimental.session.compacting` hook implementation in `src/index.ts` line by line. Treat this as the most novel feature of the phase: capture exactly what guidance is injected, when it runs, and how that compares with Public's structural compaction plugin in `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js`.
56000:9. Only after the wrapper analysis, read `external/opencode-mnemosyne-main/README.md`. Use it to extract hybrid-search claims, model choice, AGENTS.md guidance, offline-first positioning, and the stated project/global collection model. Distinguish clearly between wrapper-verified behavior and backend claims that are only documented here.
56002:11. Compare Mnemosyne directly against current `Code_Environment/Public` code: `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts` for existing hybrid retrieval, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts` for session bootstrap/resume behavior, `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js` for compaction injection, and `.opencode/skill/mcp-coco-index/README.md` for semantic code-search positioning.
56003:12. Resolve cross-phase boundaries explicitly. Do not redo phase `001` as generic FTS5 study or phase `003` as generic BM25 study. This phase owns wrapper-to-backend boundaries, vector-search implications, OpenCode plugin ergonomics, project/global scoping, AGENTS guidance, and memory-awareness compaction behavior.
56010:    Research the external repository at /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/opencode-mnemosyne-main and identify concrete improvements for Code_Environment/Public, especially around hybrid search (BM25 + vector embeddings), OpenCode plugin architecture, memory scoping (project vs global), session compaction resilience, and offline-first memory design.
56022:4. What exactly does the compaction hook preserve, and how does prompt-level tool guidance differ from Public's current structural-context compaction transport?
56028:10. What are the practical implications of the offline-first design, including local model download cost, first-run latency, privacy benefits, and failure handling when the binary or model is missing?
56036:- Do examine the compaction hook as the phase's highest-priority differentiator for `Code_Environment/Public`.
56037:- Do compare against current Public files so the research reflects today's hybrid retrieval and compaction behavior.
56044:- Do not ignore the compaction hook; it is the most novel and transferable feature for `Code_Environment/Public`.
56067:**Finding: Memory-tool awareness survives compaction through prompt injection**
56069:- What it does: Mnemosyne appends a compact memory-usage instruction block during `experimental.session.compacting`, while Public's existing compaction plugin injects structural transport context rather than memory-tool reminders.
56070:- Why it matters: Public may already preserve structural state across compaction, but it does not yet remind the agent of concrete memory tools in the same direct way.
56072:- Affected area: compaction strategy, startup guidance, memory-tool ergonomics
56092:- project/global scoping model
56093:- documented hybrid search contract
56095:- compaction-hook behavior and comparison with Public
56096:- offline-first operational tradeoffs
56108:Rank findings in this order: compaction-resilience leverage, fit with Public's current hybrid retrieval stack, clarity of wrapper-vs-backend boundary, usefulness of project/global scoping ergonomics, offline-first operational value, implementation cost, and clean differentiation from phases `001` and `003`.
56114:- explicit comparison against current Public files for hybrid retrieval, session recovery, compaction injection, and CocoIndex positioning
56135:  - **Role** is specialized in hybrid search, plugin architecture, scoping, and compaction resilience
56139:  - **Examples** show strong Mnemosyne-derived findings about hybrid search contracts and compaction hooks
56142:- findings compare Mnemosyne with current Public hybrid retrieval and compaction behavior rather than a stale baseline
56143:- project/global scoping, AGENTS guidance, offline-first tradeoffs, and compaction survival are all covered directly

58201:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:12:### Finding 3: Mnemosyne’s project/global split is ergonomic, but the actual scope key is weak
58203:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:23:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne offers one fixed tool family and one fixed compaction prompt block. Public supports response `profile` values on `memory_context`, intent-to-profile auto-routing, and runtime-level profile separation in `.codex/config.toml` (`context`, `research`, `review`, `write`, etc.).
58204:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:28:### Finding 5: The compaction hook is the most transferable idea, but Public’s implementation substrate is stronger
58205:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:31:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne injects a static memory-usage checklist into `experimental.session.compacting`. Public’s compact-code-graph plugin already does compaction injection too, but through a cached bridge transport plan, with dedupe checks and a plugin status tool.
58206:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:32:- **Why it matters for us**: the novel reusable part is not “compaction hook exists” but “memory-tool reminders survive compaction directly in prompt text.” Public can add that guidance inside its existing transport-plan architecture instead of copying Mnemosyne’s thinner standalone pattern.
58207:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:36:### Finding 6: Mnemosyne’s hybrid retrieval and offline-first story are mostly backend contract, not wrapper logic
58208:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:59:- Questions addressed: tool registration, CLI bridge semantics, search/store/delete verbs, project vs global scoping, compaction prompt injection, profile separation, offline-first operational surface, Public overlap boundaries
58209:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:60:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s checked-out repo proves a thin OpenCode plugin facade over a CLI binary; the real hybrid-search mechanics remain README-level; Public already exceeds it on MCP breadth, layered routing, and profile-aware recovery; the strongest reusable idea is direct memory-tool guidance during compaction, not the backend contract itself; the strict validation command was run and reported `RESULT: PASSED` despite a read-only temp-file warning from the validator shell
58210:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:63:- What worked: reading `src/index.ts` first made the boundary obvious very quickly, and comparing it directly with Public’s tool schemas and compaction plugin prevented stale “Public lacks hybrid retrieval” conclusions
58211:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:74:You are a research specialist in hybrid search architectures (BM25 + vector embeddings), OpenCode plugin design, memory scoping patterns, and session compaction resilience. Work like a systems analyst who can separate TypeScript wrapper behavior from backend README claims, trace plugin lifecycle and hook wiring precisely, and translate OpenCode Mnemosyne's concrete design into practical improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`.
58212:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:78:Research OpenCode Mnemosyne's hybrid search architecture, plugin design patterns, and compaction survival strategies to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around combining text and vector search, OpenCode plugin integration, project-vs-global memory scoping, and preserving memory awareness across context compaction. Focus on what the external repo actually proves in `src/index.ts` and what the README describes about the Mnemosyne backend. Classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
58213:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:86:The plugin exposes five memory tools: `memory_recall`, `memory_recall_global`, `memory_store`, `memory_store_global`, and `memory_delete`. Project scope is derived from the current directory name and auto-initialized during plugin load; the global collection is initialized lazily on first global write. The wrapper shells out to the `mnemosyne` binary via `Bun.spawn`, quotes search strings to avoid FTS syntax issues, and injects compact memory-usage guidance into the compaction prompt so agents retain memory awareness after context resets.
58215:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:99:`Code_Environment/Public` already has Spec Kit Memory with a hybrid vector/BM25 memory retrieval pipeline in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts`, startup and resume flows in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts`, and CocoIndex for semantic code search through `.opencode/skill/mcp-coco-index/README.md`. It also already has a compaction-oriented transport plugin in `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js` that injects structural context during `experimental.session.compacting`.
58216:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:101:What this repo does **not** currently have is an OpenCode memory plugin that wraps a standalone local binary, a first-class project/global tool pair with Mnemosyne's lightweight ergonomics, or a memory-specific compaction hook that injects tool-usage guidance directly into the compaction prompt. Do not repeat the outdated claim that Public lacks hybrid BM25+vector memory retrieval; compare Mnemosyne against the current code, not stale assumptions.
58218:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:112:8. 58469:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:8191:- **Why it matters for us**: the transferable idea is not “compaction exists”; it is “memory-tool reminders survive compaction in plain prompt text.” Public’s substrate is stronger, but it currently emphasizes continuity payloads more than explicit memory-verb reminders.
58472:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:8231:- Questions addressed: implicit versus explicit session creation, session maintenance state, stop-time summarization, compaction survival, trusted session identity, stale-session cleanup, Public hybrid retrieval overlap
58473:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:8232:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne has no first-class session object; continuity is mostly prompt discipline plus collection scoping; compaction preserves tool awareness but not live continuity state; Public already has server-minted session IDs, working-memory continuity, cached summary validation, session-stop autosave, and stale cleanup; the exact strict validation command returned `RESULT: PASSED` despite the shell warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`

58500:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:60:You are a research specialist in hybrid search architectures (BM25 + vector embeddings), OpenCode plugin design, memory scoping patterns, and session compaction resilience. Work like a systems analyst who can separate TypeScript wrapper behavior from backend README claims, trace plugin lifecycle and hook wiring precisely, and translate OpenCode Mnemosyne's concrete design into practical improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`.
58501:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:64:Research OpenCode Mnemosyne's hybrid search architecture, plugin design patterns, and compaction survival strategies to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around combining text and vector search, OpenCode plugin integration, project-vs-global memory scoping, and preserving memory awareness across context compaction. Focus on what the external repo actually proves in `src/index.ts` and what the README describes about the Mnemosyne backend. Classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
58502:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:72:The plugin exposes five memory tools: `memory_recall`, `memory_recall_global`, `memory_store`, `memory_store_global`, and `memory_delete`. Project scope is derived from the current directory name and auto-initialized during plugin load; the global collection is initialized lazily on first global write. The wrapper shells out to the `mnemosyne` binary via `Bun.spawn`, quotes search strings to avoid FTS syntax issues, and injects compact memory-usage guidance into the compaction prompt so agents retain memory awareness after context resets.
58504:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:85:`Code_Environment/Public` already has Spec Kit Memory with a hybrid vector/BM25 memory retrieval pipeline in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts`, startup and resume flows in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts`, and CocoIndex for semantic code search through `.opencode/skill/mcp-coco-index/README.md`. It also already has a compaction-oriented transport plugin in `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js` that injects structural context during `experimental.session.compacting`.
58505:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:87:What this repo does **not** currently have is an OpenCode memory plugin that wraps a standalone local binary, a first-class project/global tool pair with Mnemosyne's lightweight ergonomics, or a memory-specific compaction hook that injects tool-usage guidance directly into the compaction prompt. Do not repeat the outdated claim that Public lacks hybrid BM25+vector memory retrieval; compare Mnemosyne against the current code, not stale assumptions.
58507:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:98:8. Examine the `experimental.session.compacting` hook implementation in `src/index.ts` line by line. Treat this as the most novel feature of the phase: capture exactly what guidance is injected, when it runs, and how that compares with Public's structural compaction plugin in `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js`.
58508:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:99:9. Only after the wrapper analysis, read `external/opencode-mnemosyne-main/README.md`. Use it to extract hybrid-search claims, model choice, AGENTS.md guidance, offline-first positioning, and the stated project/global collection model. Distinguish clearly between wrapper-verified behavior and backend claims that are only documented here.

Work like a systems analyst who can separate TypeScript wrapper behavior from backend README claims, trace plugin lifecycle and hook wiring precisely, and translate OpenCode Mnemosyne's concrete design into practical improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`.
58569:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:9:Research OpenCode Mnemosyne's hybrid search architecture, plugin design patterns, and compaction survival strategies to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around combining text and vector search, OpenCode plugin integration, project-vs-global memory scoping, and preserving memory awareness across context compaction. Focus on what the external repo actually proves in `src/index.ts` and what the README describes about the Mnemosyne backend. Classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
58570:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:17:The plugin exposes five memory tools: `memory_recall`, `memory_recall_global`, `memory_store`, `memory_store_global`, and `memory_delete`. Project scope is derived from the current directory name and auto-initialized during plugin load; the global collection is initialized lazily on first global write. The wrapper shells out to the `mnemosyne` binary via `Bun.spawn`, quotes search strings to avoid FTS syntax issues, and injects compact memory-usage guidance into the compaction prompt so agents retain memory awareness after context resets.
58572:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:30:`Code_Environment/Public` already has Spec Kit Memory with a hybrid vector/BM25 memory retrieval pipeline in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts`, startup and resume flows in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts`, and CocoIndex for semantic code search through `.opencode/skill/mcp-coco-index/README.md`. It also already has a compaction-oriented transport plugin in `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js` that injects structural context during `experimental.session.compacting`.
58573:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:32:What this repo does **not** currently have is an OpenCode memory plugin that wraps a standalone local binary, a first-class project/global tool pair with Mnemosyne's lightweight ergonomics, or a memory-specific compaction hook that injects tool-usage guidance directly into the compaction prompt. Do not repeat the outdated claim that Public lacks hybrid BM25+vector memory retrieval; compare Mnemosyne against the current code, not stale assumptions.

58648:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:4934:You are a research specialist in hybrid search architectures (BM25 + vector embeddings), OpenCode plugin design, memory scoping patterns, and session compaction resilience. Work like a systems analyst who can separate TypeScript wrapper behavior from backend README claims, trace plugin lifecycle and hook wiring precisely, and translate OpenCode Mnemosyne's concrete design into practical improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`.
58649:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:4938:Research OpenCode Mnemosyne's hybrid search architecture, plugin design patterns, and compaction survival strategies to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around combining text and vector search, OpenCode plugin integration, project-vs-global memory scoping, and preserving memory awareness across context compaction. Focus on what the external repo actually proves in `src/index.ts` and what the README describes about the Mnemosyne backend. Classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
58650:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:4946:The plugin exposes five memory tools: `memory_recall`, `memory_recall_global`, `memory_store`, `memory_store_global`, and `memory_delete`. Project scope is derived from the current directory name and auto-initialized during plugin load; the global collection is initialized lazily on first global write. The wrapper shells out to the `mnemosyne` binary via `Bun.spawn`, quotes search strings to avoid FTS syntax issues, and injects compact memory-usage guidance into the compaction prompt so agents retain memory awareness after context resets.
58652:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:4959:`Code_Environment/Public` already has Spec Kit Memory with a hybrid vector/BM25 memory retrieval pipeline in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts`, startup and resume flows in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts`, and CocoIndex for semantic code search through `.opencode/skill/mcp-coco-index/README.md`. It also already has a compaction-oriented transport plugin in `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js` that injects structural context during `experimental.session.compacting`.
58653:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:4961:What this repo does **not** currently have is an OpenCode memory plugin that wraps a standalone local binary, a first-class project/global tool pair with Mnemosyne's lightweight ergonomics, or a memory-specific compaction hook that injects tool-usage guidance directly into the compaction prompt. Do not repeat the outdated claim that Public lacks hybrid BM25+vector memory retrieval; compare Mnemosyne against the current code, not stale assumptions.
58655:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:4972:8. Examine the `experimental.session.compacting` hook implementation in `src/index.ts` line by line. Treat this as the most novel feature of the phase: capture exactly what guidance is injected, when it runs, and how that compares with Public's structural compaction plugin in `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js`.
58656:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:4973:9. Only after the wrapper analysis, read `external/opencode-mnemosyne-main/README.md`. Resolve cross-phase boundaries explicitly. Do not redo phase `001` as generic FTS5 study or phase `003` as generic BM25 study. 58659:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:4983:    Research the external repository at /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/opencode-mnemosyne-main and identify concrete improvements for Code_Environment/Public, especially around hybrid search (BM25 + vector embeddings), OpenCode plugin architecture, memory scoping (project vs global), session compaction resilience, and offline-first memory design.

58668:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5042:- What it does: Mnemosyne appends a compact memory-usage instruction block during `experimental.session.compacting`, while Public's existing compaction plugin injects structural transport context rather than memory-tool reminders.
58669:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5043:- Why it matters: Public may already preserve structural state across compaction, but it does not yet remind the agent of concrete memory tools in the same direct way.

58683:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5292:I’ve got the public-side search stack confirmed as genuinely hybrid already: vector, FTS5/BM25, in-memory BM25, graph, and RRF fusion are all present. 58690:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5723:   656	  return autoSurfaceMemories(sessionContext.trim(), COMPACTION_TOKEN_BUDGET, 'compaction');
58692:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5932:- **What it does**: the whole plugin surface lives in one entrypoint: OpenCode loads the plugin, resolves `ctx.directory || ctx.worktree || process.cwd()`, derives a project key, shells out to `mnemosyne`, registers five tools, and adds one compaction hook. `package.json` publishes only `dist`, and there is no in-repo AGENTS file or deeper architecture layer to inspect.
58695:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5940:- **What it does**: project scope is derived from the current directory basename, normalized so literal `global` becomes `default`; project collections are auto-initialized on plugin load, while the global collection is lazily initialized only on first `memory_store_global`. The tool surface makes project/global choice explicit with separate recall/store variants.
58699:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5957:- **Why it matters for us**: we should reject any conclusion that Mnemosyne introduces hybrid retrieval as a net-new capability for Public. The real novelty is wrapper shape, scoping ergonomics, and compaction guidance.
58700:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5961:### Finding 5: The compaction hook is the strongest transferable idea because it preserves tool awareness, not just context payload
58701:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5964:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne pushes a static instruction block into `output.context` during `experimental.session.compacting`, explicitly naming `memory_recall`, `memory_store`, `memory_delete`, and the global variants. Public already preserves structural context through the compact-code-graph transport and can prepend surfaced constitutional/triggered memories during compaction, but that path does not add the same blunt “you still have these memory tools, and here is when to use them” reminder.
58702:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5965:- **Why it matters for us**: this is a low-cost, high-leverage addition for compaction resilience. Structural continuity and surfaced memories help, but direct tool reminders reduce the chance that the agent forgets to recall/store/delete after a context reset.
58705:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5973:- **Why it matters for us**: the discipline pattern is worth adopting in Public prompts and compaction guidance, but merging memory retrieval with semantic code search would blur two distinct object types: human/project memory versus code discovery.
58706:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5992:- Questions addressed: hybrid retrieval boundary, model/runtime implications, project-vs-global scoping, compaction survival, AGENTS guidance, CocoIndex separation, plugin-vs-MCP adoption shape, offline-first tradeoffs
58707:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5993:- Questions answered: the wrapper is thin; hybrid ranking is README-level not wrapper-owned; Public already has deeper hybrid retrieval; project/global split is useful but collision-prone; compaction tool reminders are the best adopt-now idea; CocoIndex should stay separate from memory retrieval
58708:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5998:- What worked: reading in the mandated order exposed the true architecture quickly; comparing wrapper code against Public’s live hybrid and compaction code prevented stale conclusions
58710:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:6002:Investigate lifecycle ergonomics in more detail: document ID flow from recall to delete, basename-collision risks for project scoping, whether Public should add a dedicated memory-tool compaction reminder block, and whether an optional OpenCode memory plugin facade is worth prototyping on top of the existing MCP-backed memory system.
58712:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:6019:- **What it does**: the whole plugin surface lives in one entrypoint: OpenCode loads the plugin, resolves `ctx.directory || ctx.worktree || process.cwd()`, derives a project key, shells out to `mnemosyne`, registers five tools, and adds one compaction hook. `package.json` publishes only `dist`, and there is no in-repo AGENTS file or deeper architecture layer to inspect.
58715:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:6027:- **What it does**: project scope is derived from the current directory basename, normalized so literal `global` becomes `default`; project collections are auto-initialized on plugin load, while the global collection is lazily initialized only on first `memory_store_global`. The tool surface makes project/global choice explicit with separate recall/store variants.
58719:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:6044:- **Why it matters for us**: we should reject any conclusion that Mnemosyne introduces hybrid retrieval as a net-new capability for Public. The real novelty is wrapper shape, scoping ergonomics, and compaction guidance.
58720:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:6048:### Finding 5: The compaction hook is the strongest transferable idea because it preserves tool awareness, not just context payload
58721:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:6051:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne pushes a static instruction block into `output.context` during `experimental.session.compacting`, explicitly naming `memory_recall`, `memory_store`, `memory_delete`, and the global variants. Public already preserves structural context through the compact-code-graph transport and can prepend surfaced constitutional/triggered memories during compaction, but that path does not add the same blunt “you still have these memory tools, and here is when to use them” reminder.
58722:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:6052:- **Why it matters for us**: this is a low-cost, high-leverage addition for compaction resilience. Structural continuity and surfaced memories help, but direct tool reminders reduce the chance that the agent forgets to recall/store/delete after a context reset.
58725:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:6060:- **Why it matters for us**: the discipline pattern is worth adopting in Public prompts and compaction guidance, but merging memory retrieval with semantic code search would blur two distinct object types: human/project memory versus code discovery.
58726:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:6079:- Questions addressed: hybrid retrieval boundary, model/runtime implications, project-vs-global scoping, compaction survival, AGENTS guidance, CocoIndex separation, plugin-vs-MCP adoption shape, offline-first tradeoffs
58727:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:6080:- Questions answered: the wrapper is thin; hybrid ranking is README-level not wrapper-owned; Public already has deeper hybrid retrieval; project/global split is useful but collision-prone; compaction tool reminders are the best adopt-now idea; CocoIndex should stay separate from memory retrieval
58728:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:6085:- What worked: reading in the mandated order exposed the true architecture quickly; comparing wrapper code against Public’s live hybrid and compaction code prevented stale conclusions
58730:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:6089:Investigate lifecycle ergonomics in more detail: document ID flow from recall to delete, basename-collision risks for project scoping, whether Public should add a dedicated memory-tool compaction reminder block, and whether an optional OpenCode memory plugin facade is worth prototyping on top of the existing MCP-backed memory system.
58735:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:32:- **What it does**: project scope is derived from the current directory basename, project memory is auto-initialized on plugin load, global memory is lazily initialized on first write, and the tool surface makes project/global recall feel explicit and lightweight.
58736:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:57:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s wrapper proves only a thin search bridge; its hybrid ranking claims are README-level; Public already has deeper hybrid retrieval with query routing, lexical weighting, adaptive fusion, and fallback tiers; the most transferable search idea is simpler project/global memory tool ergonomics, not the backend retrieval stack; the exact strict validation command returned `RESULT: PASSED` with the warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`
58737:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:71:You are a research specialist in hybrid search architectures (BM25 + vector embeddings), OpenCode plugin design, memory scoping patterns, and session compaction resilience. Work like a systems analyst who can separate TypeScript wrapper behavior from backend README claims, trace plugin lifecycle and hook wiring precisely, and translate OpenCode Mnemosyne's concrete design into practical improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`.
58738:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:75:Research OpenCode Mnemosyne's hybrid search architecture, plugin design patterns, and compaction survival strategies to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around combining text and vector search, OpenCode plugin integration, project-vs-global memory scoping, and preserving memory awareness across context compaction. Focus on what the external repo actually proves in `src/index.ts` and what the README describes about the Mnemosyne backend. Classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
58739:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:83:The plugin exposes five memory tools: `memory_recall`, `memory_recall_global`, `memory_store`, `memory_store_global`, and `memory_delete`. Project scope is derived from the current directory name and auto-initialized during plugin load; the global collection is initialized lazily on first global write. The wrapper shells out to the `mnemosyne` binary via `Bun.spawn`, quotes search strings to avoid FTS syntax issues, and injects compact memory-usage guidance into the compaction prompt so agents retain memory awareness after context resets.
58741:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:96:`Code_Environment/Public` already has Spec Kit Memory with a hybrid vector/BM25 memory retrieval pipeline in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts`, startup and resume flows in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts`, and CocoIndex for semantic code search through `.opencode/skill/mcp-coco-index/README.md`. It also already has a compaction-oriented transport plugin in `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js` that injects structural context during `experimental.session.compacting`.
58742:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:98:What this repo does **not** currently have is an OpenCode memory plugin that wraps a standalone local binary, a first-class project/global tool pair with Mnemosyne's lightweight ergonomics, or a memory-specific compaction hook that injects tool-usage guidance directly into the compaction prompt. Do not repeat the outdated claim that Public lacks hybrid BM25+vector memory retrieval; compare Mnemosyne against the current code, not stale assumptions.
58744:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:109:8. Examine the `experimental.session.compacting` hook implementation in `src/index.ts` line by line. Treat this as the most novel feature of the phase: capture exactly what guidance is injected, when it runs, and how that compares with Public's structural compaction plugin in `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js`.
58745:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:110:9. Only after the wrapper analysis, read `external/opencode-mnemosyne-main/README.md`. Use it to extract hybrid-search claims, model choice, AGENTS.md guidance, offline-first positioning, and the stated project/global collection model. Distinguish clearly between wrapper-verified behavior and backend claims that are only documented here.
58746:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:112:11. Compare Mnemosyne directly against current `Code_Environment/Public` code: `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts` for existing hybrid retrieval, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts` for session bootstrap/resume behavior, `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js` for compaction injection, and `.opencode/skill/mcp-coco-index/README.md` for semantic code-search positioning.
58747:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:113:12. Resolve cross-phase boundaries explicitly. Do not redo phase `001` as generic FTS5 study or phase `003` as generic BM25 study. This phase owns wrapper-to-backend boundaries, vector-search implications, OpenCode plugin ergonomics, project/global scoping, AGENTS guidance, and memory-awareness compaction behavior.
58748:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:120:    Research the external repository at /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/opencode-mnemosyne-main and identify concrete improvements for Code_Environment/Public, especially around hybrid search (BM25 + vector embeddings), OpenCode plugin architecture, memory scoping (project vs global), session compaction resilience, and offline-first memory design.
58750:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:132:4. What exactly does the compaction hook preserve, and how does prompt-level tool guidance differ from Public's current structural-context compaction transport?
58753:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:146:- Do examine the compaction hook as the phase's highest-priority differentiator for `Code_Environment/Public`.

58757:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:179:- What it does: Mnemosyne appends a compact memory-usage instruction block during `experimental.session.compacting`, while Public's existing compaction plugin injects structural transport context rather than memory-tool reminders.
58758:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:180:- Why it matters: Public may already preserve structural state across compaction, but it does not yet remind the agent of concrete memory tools in the same direct way.

58797:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:5742:### Finding 5: The compaction hook is Mnemosyne’s best hygiene idea, but it is guidance-only

Public’s compaction plugin injects transport content blocks, while server instructions advertise key tools but do not inject the same direct memory-discipline checklist into compaction.
58802:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:5780:- Questions addressed: dedup/upsert behavior, revision tracking, delete semantics, garbage collection, soft deletes, topic key stability, compaction hygiene guidance, auditability of mutation flows

Public’s compaction plugin injects transport content blocks, while server instructions advertise key tools but do not inject the same direct memory-discipline checklist into compaction.
58815:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:5867:- Questions addressed: dedup/upsert behavior, revision tracking, delete semantics, garbage collection, soft deletes, topic key stability, compaction hygiene guidance, auditability of mutation flows
58816:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:5868:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s wrapper exposes no dedup, no upsert, no revision lineage, no soft-delete lifecycle, and no retention/GC contract; contradiction hygiene is primarily agent guidance plus hard delete; basename-derived project scoping is materially less stable than Public’s canonical-path plus logical-key approach; the compaction reminder block is the only clear hygiene improvement worth borrowing immediately; the exact strict validation command returned `RESULT: PASSED` with the warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`
58819:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:3:- **What it does**: Wrapper-confirmed, the project collection is auto-initialized on plugin load, while the global collection is initialized only on first global write. That asymmetry makes project memory frictionless and global memory explicit. Public already supports broader local/global retrieval semantics through `retrievalLevel: "local" | "global" | "auto"`, but not via a similarly simple project/global tool pair.
58820:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:8:### Finding 5: Mnemosyne’s strongest agent-integration idea is a repeated memory-discipline checklist across AGENTS and compaction
58821:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:10:- **What it does**: Both wrapper-confirmed and README-documented, the same behavioral rules show up twice: search when history matters, store concise decisions, delete contradictions, reserve `core` for always-needed facts, and use global only for cross-project preferences. Public already injects startup and compaction notes, but those notes are more about recovery and structural context than a short memory-discipline checklist.
58822:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:15:### Finding 6: Mnemosyne’s hybrid search story stays behind the backend contract, so Public should keep memory retrieval and semantic code search separate
58824:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:40:- Questions addressed: agent-agnostic plugin shape, passive capture vs manual capture, multi-agent safety, project/global scoping ergonomics, AGENTS guidance, compaction survival, hybrid-search boundary, CocoIndex separation, offline/local integration assumptions.
58825:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:41:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s wrapper is intentionally minimal and portable, but it delegates nearly all safe behavior to agent discipline; it has no passive capture or actor-scoped governance, its best transferable idea is the repeated memory-discipline checklist across AGENTS and compaction, and its project-first/global-lazy ergonomics are useful even though the underlying scope model is too weak for Public.
58827:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:49:Trace the adapter contract for an external-binary memory facade that preserves Public-grade multi-agent safety: required structured response envelope, actor/session scope propagation, project-first/global escalation rules, mutation confirmation semantics, and a shared memory-discipline checklist injected at both startup and compaction.
58828:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:56:You are a research specialist in hybrid search architectures (BM25 + vector embeddings), OpenCode plugin design, memory scoping patterns, and session compaction resilience. Work like a systems analyst who can separate TypeScript wrapper behavior from backend README claims, trace plugin lifecycle and hook wiring precisely, and translate OpenCode Mnemosyne's concrete design into practical improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`.
58829:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:60:Research OpenCode Mnemosyne's hybrid search architecture, plugin design patterns, and compaction survival strategies to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around combining text and vector search, OpenCode plugin integration, project-vs-global memory scoping, and preserving memory awareness across context compaction. Focus on what the external repo actually proves in `src/index.ts` and what the README describes about the Mnemosyne backend. Classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
58830:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:68:The plugin exposes five memory tools: `memory_recall`, `memory_recall_global`, `memory_store`, `memory_store_global`, and `memory_delete`. Project scope is derived from the current directory name and auto-initialized during plugin load; the global collection is initialized lazily on first global write. The wrapper shells out to the `mnemosyne` binary via `Bun.spawn`, quotes search strings to avoid FTS syntax issues, and injects compact memory-usage guidance into the compaction prompt so agents retain memory awareness after context resets.
58832:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:81:`Code_Environment/Public` already has Spec Kit Memory with a hybrid vector/BM25 memory retrieval pipeline in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts`, startup and resume flows in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts`, and CocoIndex for semantic code search through `.opencode/skill/mcp-coco-index/README.md`. It also already has a compaction-oriented transport plugin in `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js` that injects structural context during `experimental.session.compacting`.
58833:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:83:What this repo does **not** currently have is an OpenCode memory plugin that wraps a standalone local binary, a first-class project/global tool pair with Mnemosyne's lightweight ergonomics, or a memory-specific compaction hook that injects tool-usage guidance directly into the compaction prompt. Do not repeat the outdated claim that Public lacks hybrid BM25+vector memory retrieval; compare Mnemosyne against the current code, not stale assumptions.
58835:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:94:8. Examine the `experimental.session.compacting` hook implementation in `src/index.ts` line by line. Treat this as the most novel feature of the phase: capture exactly what guidance is injected, when it runs, and how that compares with Public's structural compaction plugin in `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js`.
58836:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:95:9. Only after the wrapper analysis, read `external/opencode-mnemosyne-main/README.md`. 58922:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:4519:### Finding 5: Mnemosyne’s compaction hook preserves memory awareness, but not live retrieval readiness or routing
58924:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:4521:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne injects a static checklist telling the agent to recall/store/delete memory. 58926:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:4533:### Finding 7: Mnemosyne’s offline bridge lacks an explicit retrieval-readiness contract
58928:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:4535:- **What it does**: Missing binary handling returns a plain error string, project init silently swallows failures, and the README documents a first-run model download of about 500 MB. Public exposes explicit bootstrap, health, graph, and code-search readiness surfaces instead of making retrieval availability implicit.
58930:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:4558:- Questions addressed: wrapper-visible hybrid-search contract, query handoff opacity, retrieval scope ergonomics, session-aware continuity, compaction-time retrieval guidance, package/audit boundary, offline readiness and failure handling.
58932:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:4566:Define an external-binary adapter contract for Public that preserves structured retrieval envelopes, trusted session propagation, readiness/health signaling, and optional project/global UX aliases without collapsing `memory_search`, CocoIndex, and code-graph into a single opaque recall surface.
58935:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:4593:### Finding 3: Public’s `retrievalLevel` is a richer retrieval control than Mnemosyne’s separate project/global recall tools
58936:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:4596:- **Why it matters for us**: The transferable idea is ergonomic aliasing, not a backend gap. If we ever add a project/global façade, it should sit on top of `memory_search`, not replace it.
58938:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:4607:### Finding 5: Mnemosyne’s compaction hook preserves memory awareness, but not live retrieval readiness or routing
58940:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:4609:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne injects a static checklist telling the agent to recall/store/delete memory. Public’s compaction and bootstrap surfaces already inject runtime-specific structural status and route code-search questions toward CocoIndex or `code_graph_query`.
58942:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:4621:### Finding 7: Mnemosyne’s offline bridge lacks an explicit retrieval-readiness contract
58944:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:4623:- **What it does**: Missing binary handling returns a plain error string, project init silently swallows failures, and the README documents a first-run model download of about 500 MB. Public exposes explicit bootstrap, health, graph, and code-search readiness surfaces instead of making retrieval availability implicit.
58946:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:4646:- Questions addressed: wrapper-visible hybrid-search contract, query handoff opacity, retrieval scope ergonomics, session-aware continuity, compaction-time retrieval guidance, package/audit boundary, offline readiness and failure handling.
58948:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:4654:Define an external-binary adapter contract for Public that preserves structured retrieval envelopes, trusted session propagation, readiness/health signaling, and optional project/global UX aliases without collapsing `memory_search`, CocoIndex, and code-graph into a single opaque recall surface.
58950:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:8:### Finding 5: The compaction hook is Mnemosyne’s best hygiene idea, but it is guidance-only
58951:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10:- **What it does**: Wrapper-confirmed, Mnemosyne injects a compact “Persistent Memory” block during `experimental.session.compacting` that reminds the agent when to recall, store, delete contradicted entries, use `core`, and use global scope. Public’s compaction plugin injects transport content blocks, while server instructions advertise key tools but do not inject the same direct memory-discipline checklist into compaction.
58955:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:46:- Questions addressed: dedup/upsert behavior, revision tracking, delete semantics, garbage collection, soft deletes, topic key stability, compaction hygiene guidance, auditability of mutation flows
58956:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:47:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s wrapper exposes no dedup, no upsert, no revision lineage, no soft-delete lifecycle, and no retention/GC contract; contradiction hygiene is primarily agent guidance plus hard delete; basename-derived project scoping is materially less stable than Public’s canonical-path plus logical-key approach; the compaction reminder block is the only clear hygiene improvement worth borrowing immediately; the exact strict validation command returned `RESULT: PASSED` with the warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`
58958:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:61:You are a research specialist in hybrid search architectures (BM25 + vector embeddings), OpenCode plugin design, memory scoping patterns, and session compaction resilience. Work like a systems analyst who can separate TypeScript wrapper behavior from backend README claims, trace plugin lifecycle and hook wiring precisely, and translate OpenCode Mnemosyne's concrete design into practical improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`.
58959:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:65:Research OpenCode Mnemosyne's hybrid search architecture, plugin design patterns, and compaction survival strategies to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around combining text and vector search, OpenCode plugin integration, project-vs-global memory scoping, and preserving memory awareness across context compaction. Focus on what the external repo actually proves in `src/index.ts` and what the README describes about the Mnemosyne backend. Classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
58960:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:73:The plugin exposes five memory tools: `memory_recall`, `memory_recall_global`, `memory_store`, `memory_store_global`, and `memory_delete`. Project scope is derived from the current directory name and auto-initialized during plugin load; the global collection is initialized lazily on first global write. The wrapper shells out to the `mnemosyne` binary via `Bun.spawn`, quotes search strings to avoid FTS syntax issues, and injects compact memory-usage guidance into the compaction prompt so agents retain memory awareness after context resets.
58962:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:86:`Code_Environment/Public` already has Spec Kit Memory with a hybrid vector/BM25 memory retrieval pipeline in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts`, startup and resume flows in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts`, and CocoIndex for semantic code search through `.opencode/skill/mcp-coco-index/README.md`. It also already has a compaction-oriented transport plugin in `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js` that injects structural context during `experimental.session.compacting`.
58963:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:88:What this repo does **not** currently have is an OpenCode memory plugin that wraps a standalone local binary, a first-class project/global tool pair with Mnemosyne's lightweight ergonomics, or a memory-specific compaction hook that injects tool-usage guidance directly into the compaction prompt. Do not repeat the outdated claim that Public lacks hybrid BM25+vector memory retrieval; compare Mnemosyne against the current code, not stale assumptions.
58965:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:99:8. Examine the `experimental.session.compacting` hook implementation in `src/index.ts` line by line. Treat this as the most novel feature of the phase: capture exactly what guidance is injected, when it runs, and how that compares with Public's structural compaction plugin in `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js`.
58966:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:100:9. Only after the wrapper analysis, read `external/opencode-mnemosyne-main/README.md`.
