# Iteration 021: CROSS-SYSTEM PATTERNS

## Focus
CROSS-SYSTEM PATTERNS: Compare patterns from this system against the other 4 research phases. What converges across all systems? What's unique to this one?

## Findings
### Finding 1: The strongest convergence is behavioral continuity at runtime seams, not better ranking math
- **Source**: [Mnemosyne wrapper]( /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L208), [Public compaction plugin]( /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js#L396), [Engram iteration]( /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md#L15713), [Mex iteration]( /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-014.md#L9303), [Modus iteration]( /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-013.md#L36), [MemPalace iteration]( /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md#L5411)
- **What it does**: Across all five systems, the durable value shows up at startup, resume, compaction, save timing, or repair timing. Engram uses hook/setup injection, Mex uses a control-plane loop, Modus pushes toward execution-readiness, MemPalace emphasizes pre-compaction preservation, and Mnemosyne injects a direct memory action card during `experimental.session.compacting`.
- **Why it matters**: The cross-system consensus is that the next leverage point for Public is agent behavior under context loss or drift, not another retrieval channel.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 2: Every serious system grows a control plane above raw storage, but Mnemosyne is the thinnest version of that pattern
- **Source**: [Engram iteration]( /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md#L15692), [Mex iteration]( /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-014.md#L9289), [Modus iteration]( /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-013.md#L8), [MemPalace iteration]( /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md#L5418), [Mnemosyne README]( /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L62)
- **What it does**: Engram has explicit session objects, Mex has a truth-audit and repair loop, Modus has governance/proposal/readiness planes, and MemPalace has protocol/status surfaces. Mnemosyne only adds AGENTS guidance plus a compaction reminder; it does not add a richer operator-facing control plane.
- **Why it matters**: This makes Mnemosyne useful as a minimum viable behavioral layer, but not as the architectural ceiling. Public’s gap is broader: it still lacks a dedicated stale-truth/remediation plane more than it lacks retrieval features.
- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 3: Scope separation converges, but Mnemosyne’s project/global split is uniquely simple and uniquely weak
- **Source**: [Mnemosyne index.ts]( /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L27), [Mnemosyne README]( /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L51), [Modus iteration]( /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-013.md#L8), [MemPalace iteration]( /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md#L5439), [Public memory search]( /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L14)
- **What it does**: All systems partition memory somehow: Engram by agent/admin and sessions, Mex by scaffold vs repair plane, Modus by separate planes, MemPalace by layers and fact plane. Mnemosyne is the simplest: basename-derived project collection, a single global collection, plus `core` tagging.
- **Why it matters**: The ergonomic lesson is real, but the identity model is brittle. Mnemosyne’s split is useful as a UX alias, not as a storage/governance model for Public.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Impact**: medium

### Finding 4: Mnemosyne is uniquely valuable as a thin OpenCode-native facade over a local backend, not as a retrieval-engine replacement
- **Source**: [Mnemosyne package]( /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/package.json#L1), [Mnemosyne README]( /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L80), [Public startup]( /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L1495), [Public compaction plugin]( /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js#L296), [CocoIndex README]( /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/mcp-coco-index/README.md#L42)
- **What it does**: The checked-out Mnemosyne repo is mostly a Bun/OpenCode wrapper plus README contract. Its BM25/vector/RRF story is documented, but the wrapper itself mainly contributes CLI bridging, tool exposure, and compaction guidance. Public already has visible hybrid search and separate semantic code search.
- **Why it matters**: Cross-system, the unique contribution here is facade ergonomics: an easy local memory plugin with a tight OpenCode surface. It is not evidence for reworking Public’s retrieval core.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 5: Readiness and trust boundaries are a hidden convergence theme, and Mnemosyne is uniquely brittle there
- **Source**: [Mnemosyne index.ts]( /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L78), [Mnemosyne README]( /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L18), [Public startup]( /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L1589), [Mex iteration]( /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-014.md#L9310), [Modus iteration]( /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-013.md#L15)
- **What it does**: The other systems all expose some visible posture around truth, trust, freshness, or readiness. Mnemosyne auto-inits collections and depends on a first-use model download, but the wrapper does not fail closed on init readiness and can still advertise memory usage at compaction even if the backend is broken.
- **Why it matters**: This is the biggest unique warning from this system. If we borrow its ergonomics, we should also invert its failure posture and keep readiness explicit and health-gated.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: high
